At our residential addiction treatment centre, we do not follow a pre-defined approach to treating morphine addiction. Instead, we advocate a fully pragmatic approach to addiction—using whatever works best to help you achieve your recovery goals. This means we fully support medication-assisted treatment (MAT) and do not require you to stop using other substances (such as nicotine or alcohol) during the process.
Every person who comes to our centre receives a fully personalised treatment plan, designed according to their specific needs. All of our morphine addiction programmes include a carefully monitored, medically supervised withdrawal process. We also aim to make this process as comfortable as possible through supportive IV infusions and a range of complementary therapies.
Morphine.section1Description:
Our treatment programmes are built on empathy, innovation, and a practical approach, recognising that every person’s recovery journey is different. In addition to a medically safe morphine withdrawal process, which may include medication-assisted therapies to help manage withdrawal symptoms, a key element of our programme is the use of extensive assessments and testing.
To design your individual treatment plan, we begin with a comprehensive medical and psychiatric evaluation, which allows us to identify any physical, emotional, or psychological factors that may be contributing to your morphine dependence. This detailed assessment helps us address the root causes of the problem and guide you toward a healthier and more fulfilling life.
To treat morphine dependence, we integrate psychotherapeutic techniques such as Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) and Dialectical Behaviour Therapy (DBT) with modern therapies like biofeedback and IV Nutrient Therapy, as well as mindfulness-based techniques such as Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), with the aim of supporting lasting recovery.
Beyond addressing the root causes of addiction, we also focus on its wider impact, including potential neurological or physical damage associated with morphine abuse. We treat these aspects alongside the underlying factors driving addiction. Our approach integrates intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, all working together to reduce stress and support long-term, sustainable recovery.
Morphine.section2Description:
We are the only residential addiction treatment centre in the world that assigns dedicated professionals to each patient.
Throughout the morphine withdrawal process and all stages of treatment, you will be supported by a team of experts, including doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists. A key member of this team is a live-in therapist, available to provide 24/7 emotional support throughout your entire stay.
Together, the team provides round-the-clock care and treatment to give you the best possible chance of achieving a strong and lasting recovery. Regardless of the combination of therapies you receive, they will all be delivered with the high level of medical excellence that defines our approach.
Morphine.section3Description:
We tailor our comprehensive and intensive morphine addiction treatment programmes to meet your specific needs. What all of our programmes have in common is that they are deeply guided by our core values of pragmatism and empathy.
At our prescription drug addiction treatment centre in Querétaro, we provide treatment for morphine addiction, as well as for any co-occurring dependencies or mental health conditions that may be present.

Fentanyl.conditionHeading: Fentanyl Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
Fentanyl.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we can help you overcome your fentanyl addiction, no matter how difficult it may seem. Through a holistic and pragmatic approach, our personalised treatment programmes focus on providing a safe, expert-supervised withdrawal process, while also addressing the underlying causes that may be contributing to your addiction.
Fentanyl.whatIsDescription:
Fentanyl is a narcotic opioid used as a painkiller that can be 50 to 100 times more potent than morphine. This powerful medication works by reducing or disconnecting the perception of pain. It is most commonly prescribed to treat severe pain associated with major surgery, cancer, or nerve damage.
In recent years, there has been a significant increase in fentanyl overdoses, largely because this substance is often mixed with or sold as other drugs, such as prescription opioids or heroin. Due to its extreme potency, the risk of overdose is extremely high, and in some cases even a small amount can be fatal. Fentanyl is also highly addictive, which means that what begins as a legitimate medical use can quickly become problematic as tolerance develops.
Physical dependence occurs when a person begins to rely on fentanyl to an excessive degree. The body becomes so accustomed to the presence of the substance that, when it is absent, it perceives that something essential is missing, which leads to withdrawal symptoms. Psychological dependence, on the other hand, develops when fentanyl is used to numb or escape from emotional pain such as stress, loneliness, or traumatic experiences. Many people struggling with prescription medication addiction experience varying levels of both physical and psychological dependence at the same time.
Over time, fentanyl abuse can lead to serious health consequences. It can increase the risk of respiratory depression, liver and kidney damage, seizures, and may also contribute to the development of mental health disorders or other substance use problems.
Due to its highly addictive nature, it can be very difficult to stop using fentanyl without professional support and structured treatment. In many cases, the safest way to do this is through a medically supervised detox, which helps manage the physical withdrawal symptoms while working with mental health professionals to address the psychological aspects of the process.
Fentanyl.signsTitle: What are the signs of fentanyl addiction?
Fentanyl.signsDescription: Recognising the signs of fentanyl addiction is essential in order to seek help in time. This powerful opioid can severely affect a person’s physical health, emotional well-being, and behaviour. As dependence develops, noticeable changes often appear in habits, mood, and relationships. Identifying these warning signs early can be key to beginning the recovery process.
Fentanyl.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of fentanyl addiction
Fentanyl.symptom1Item1: Wanting to cut down or stop using fentanyl but feeling unable to.
Fentanyl.symptom1Item2: Ingesting fentanyl even though your pain has subsided.
Fentanyl.symptom1Item3: Lying or exaggerating pain to get prescribed fentanyl.
Fentanyl.symptom1Item4: Taking fentanyl in response to stress, anxiety or boredom.
Fentanyl.symptom1Item5: Spending less time with family, friends or doing activities you once enjoyed.
Fentanyl.symptom1Item6: Going to different doctors or chemists in an attempt to get fentanyl.
Fentanyl.symptom1Item7: Needing ever larger doses to get the same pleasurable effects.
Fentanyl.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of fentanyl addiction?
Fentanyl.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of fentanyl addiction?
Fentanyl.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of fentanyl addiction?
Fentanyl.symptom4Item1: Frequent and unexplained absences from work, school, or social commitments.
Fentanyl.symptom4Item2: Neglect of responsibilities, relationships, or personal well-being.
Fentanyl.symptom4Item4: Feeling as though fentanyl has taken over your life.
Fentanyl.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot tell you exactly what your four-week stay will involve, as each person’s journey with fentanyl addiction is unique. At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that no two recovery paths are the same, which is why every individual requires a fully personalised treatment plan designed to meet their specific needs. At the same time, it is important to emphasise that we do not follow a rigid treatment model. Instead, we advocate a fully pragmatic approach to addiction, using whatever works best to help you achieve your goals.
Each of your days with us will be carefully structured and will include a variety of handpicked therapies specifically designed to address your fentanyl dependency and help you regain control of your life. This may include treatments such as intravenous infusions (IVs) alongside other specialised therapies to make the withdrawal process as comfortable and safe as possible. All the therapies we use complement one another, addressing not only the physical aspects of addiction but also the emotional and psychological factors involved. See below for more details.
Fentanyl.section1Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that every journey is unique, which is why we never adopt a “one-size-fits-all” approach. Instead, we tailor each treatment programme to your individual needs. While we use innovative techniques and a holistic model of care, it is your personal circumstances and goals that guide everything we do.
Our care goes far beyond simply guiding you through the withdrawal process or managing symptoms. We take the time to conduct thorough assessments and testing to identify the root causes of your fentanyl dependency. With this understanding, we design a personalised programme aimed at helping you overcome it in a lasting way. Your comprehensive, bespoke treatment plan combines a range of therapies, including biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), bioresonance, Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, acupuncture, and equine-assisted therapy.
In every case, our treatment goes beyond addressing only the causes of fentanyl addiction. Alongside treating the underlying factors that have contributed to the dependency, we also focus on the effects it may have caused, such as potential neurological or physical damage. Addressing these aspects typically involves intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, all aimed at reducing stress and supporting strong, sustainable long-term recovery.
Fentanyl.section2Description:
We are the only residential addiction treatment centre in the world that assigns dedicated professionals to each patient.
Throughout your withdrawal process and during every stage of treatment, you will be supported by a team of experts. This team includes doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists, who work together to provide round-the-clock care and treatment. A key member of this team is a live-in therapist, available to offer continuous emotional support throughout your entire stay.
Regardless of the combination of therapies you receive, they will all be delivered with the high level of medical excellence that defines our approach.
Fentanyl.section3Description:
Our fentanyl addiction treatment programmes are designed bespoke down to the last detail. Guided by our core principles of pragmatism and empathy, we meet you where you are and tailor the programme to suit your lifestyle, needs, and goals.
At our private addiction treatment centre in Querétaro, we provide treatment for fentanyl addiction, as well as for any co-occurring dependencies or mental health conditions that may be present.

Ghb.conditionHeading: GHB Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
Ghb.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we are here to help you overcome GHB addiction, no matter how overwhelming it may seem. Our approach is compassionate, practical, and non-judgmental. We will support you every step of the way, providing you with the tools you need to detox safely, manage your symptoms, and address the deeper issues underlying your addiction.
Ghb.whatIsDescription:
GHB (Gamma-Hydroxybutyrate) is a central nervous system depressant that produces a relaxing effect similar to alcohol. It is also known as “Liquid Ecstasy” or “G.” In high doses, it can cause an inability to move, memory loss, and blackouts. These effects have led to its use in sexual assaults. Being colorless and odorless, it can be easily added to drinks without detection. In smaller doses, its effects are similar to a stimulant, producing euphoria, energy, and sociability. Once consumed, the effects of GHB appear within approximately 15 to 30 minutes and can last up to 6 hours.
GHB drastically increases the amount of dopamine circulating in the brain and central nervous system, which explains why it can be highly addictive. Over time, the brain can come to rely on the substance to regulate dopamine levels, so the absence of GHB leads to progressively lower levels of this neurotransmitter. Additionally, it can create a physical dependency similar in severity and risk to that of benzodiazepines or alcohol. For this reason, GHB abuse can result in numerous withdrawal symptoms and other negative health effects.
At Gabriel Arcángel, we combine medical, psychological, and personalised care to treat GHB addiction in a truly holistic way. We are proud to be the only clinic in the world that addresses co-occurring conditions and explores every possible root cause of addiction. This approach allows us to help you achieve meaningful recovery in weeks, not months or years.
Ghb.signsTitle: What are the signs of GHB addiction?
Ghb.signsDescription: Recognizing the signs of GHB addiction is crucial for seeking help in time. This substance can profoundly affect a person’s physical health, emotional state, and behavior. As dependence develops, noticeable changes often appear in habits, mood, and personal relationships. Identifying these early signs can help facilitate the start of a recovery process.
Ghb.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of GHB addiction
Ghb.symptom1Item1: Being unable to stop using GHB despite wanting to quit.
Ghb.symptom1Item2: Becoming tolerant to GHB, meaning you need increasingly larger doses of GHB to achieve the same high.
Ghb.symptom1Item3: Feeling you need the drug to feel “normal”.
Ghb.symptom1Item4: Being fixated on obtaining and taking GHB.
Ghb.symptom1Item5: Neglecting responsibilities, like work or your family, in favour of taking GHB.
Ghb.symptom1Item6: Physical withdrawal symptoms such as sweating, nausea, vomiting and tremors.
Ghb.symptom1Item7: Psychological withdrawal symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, confusion, delirium and hallucinations.
Ghb.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of GHB addiction?
Ghb.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of GHB addiction?
Ghb.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of GHB addiction?
Ghb.symptom4Item5: Feeling as though GHB has taken over your life.
Ghb.howWeTreatDescription:
We can’t tell you exactly what your four-week stay will involve, as each person’s experience with GHB addiction is deeply personal. At Gabriel Arcángel, we recognise that no two recovery journeys are the same, so we create a fully bespoke treatment plan for each individual; there is no pre-defined treatment path. At the same time, it is important to emphasise that we do not follow a rigid therapeutic model. Instead, we advocate a completely pragmatic approach to addiction, using whatever works best to help you achieve your goals.
Your time with us will be carefully structured and will include a variety of therapeutic elements specifically selected to help you overcome your GHB dependency. We use supporting infusions (IVs) and various therapies to make the process as comfortable as possible. All the therapies we employ are designed to complement one another and work together, addressing not only the physical addiction but also the emotional and psychological factors involved. See below for more details.
Ghb.section1Description:
Our GHB addiction treatment adopts a multidisciplinary approach, addressing all the underlying causes of dependency. Upon arrival, we carry out a thorough assessment to understand the physical, medical, and psychological aspects of your addiction. If necessary, we will guide you through a safe, medically supervised withdrawal, overseen by our psychiatrist and supported 24/7 by our clinical team.
After detox, we provide the therapies and treatments needed to help you understand the root causes of your GHB addiction. Our goal is to identify the factors that led to the dependency, whether they are physical, emotional, or psychological. We work to restore any imbalances and support your long-term recovery through a combination of therapies, including biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive-Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, acupuncture, and art therapy.
In addition to addressing the root causes of addiction, we also focus on its wider effects, such as neurological or physical damage that can result from substance use. These are treated alongside the underlying factors contributing to the addiction. Our approach integrates intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, all with the shared aim of reducing stress and supporting your recovery.
Ghb.section2Description:
We are the only residential drug treatment centre in the world to provide dedicated professionals to each patient.
During the GHB withdrawal process and all other treatments, you will be supported by a team of experts. You will have the backing of internationally renowned doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists. A key member of this team is a live-in therapist, available to provide emotional support 24/7 throughout your entire stay. Together, they deliver continuous care and treatment to give you the best chance of achieving a solid and lasting recovery. Regardless of the unique combination of therapies you receive, all will be provided with the high standard of medical excellence for which we are known.
Ghb.section3Description:
We design our intensive and holistic treatment programmes to be tailored to your specific needs. Each programme is guided by our principles of pragmatism and empathy, providing a fully individualized treatment plan that works for you and fits seamlessly into your life.
At our discreet drug addiction treatment centre in Querétaro, we can provide treatment for GHB addiction as well as any co-occurring dependencies or mental health conditions.

PrescriptionStimulants.conditionHeading: Prescription Stimulant Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
PrescriptionStimulants.conditionDescription: We can help you overcome your prescription stimulant addiction, no matter how challenging it may feel. Through our combination of advanced diagnostics, medical excellence, and psychological treatments, we address every underlying cause of your stimulant addiction.
PrescriptionStimulants.whatIsDescription:
Prescription stimulants are medications commonly used to treat attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and narcolepsy. While highly effective for managing these conditions, they also carry a risk of misuse and dependency, especially when used outside of medical supervision or in higher doses than prescribed.
For some people, prescription stimulants can create a heightened sense of focus or energy, which can become addictive. When the stimulant helps someone escape underlying issues such as anxiety, stress, or low self-esteem, a cycle of dependency can develop. Over time, the brain begins to associate the feeling of alertness or relief with the drug, leading to a dangerous dependency. As tolerance builds, higher doses may be needed to achieve the same effects, and withdrawal symptoms make it harder to stop. Chronic use of prescription stimulants can result in cognitive impairment, mood swings, and potential long-term mental health challenges, affecting personal relationships and overall well-being.
At Gabriel Arcángel, we help those struggling with stimulant addiction through a personalised, comprehensive approach, addressing both the physical and emotional aspects of addiction to support long-term recovery.
PrescriptionStimulants.signsTitle: What are the signs of prescription stimulant addiction?
PrescriptionStimulants.signsDescription: As with other addictions, one of the clearest signs of prescription stimulant dependency is continuing to use the substance despite its negative impact on your life. Being unable to stop using stimulants even when you want to quit is a clear indicator.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of prescription stimulant addiction
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom1Item1: Continuing to use despite negative side effects such as anxiety, paranoia, or mood swings.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom1Item2: Relying on prescription stimulants to stay focused, alert, or perform everyday tasks.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom1Item3: Developing a higher tolerance, needing more of the drug to achieve the same effects.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom1Item4: Experiencing withdrawal symptoms, such as fatigue, depression, or irritability when not using.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom1Item5: Prioritising tasks or events based on whether or not stimulants are available or usable.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of prescription stimulant addiction?
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom2Item3: Rapid heartbeat or elevated blood pressure.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptoms3Title: What are the emotional symptoms of prescription stimulant addiction?
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom3Item2: Feelings of agitation or nervousness when not using.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom3Item3: Difficulty managing emotions without the stimulant.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom3Item4: Emotional withdrawal from people or activities not related to stimulant use.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of prescription stimulant addiction?
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom4Item1: Using prescription stimulants as a way to cope with stress, work pressure, or mental health challenges.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom4Item2: Experiencing anxiety, paranoia, or extreme highs and lows, especially with higher doses.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom4Item3: Struggling with concentration or cognitive function when not using.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom4Item4: Losing interest in activities or hobbies that were previously enjoyable.
PrescriptionStimulants.symptom4Item5: Justifying or rationalising use despite recognising its harmful effects on your well-being.
PrescriptionStimulants.dependenciesHeading: Types of prescription stimulant addictions we treat
PrescriptionStimulants.dependenciesDescription: We understand unequivocally that pain is universal, but recovery has to be personal.
PrescriptionStimulants.howWeTreatHeading: How we treat Prescription Stimulant Addiction
PrescriptionStimulants.howWeTreatDescription:
We can’t tell you exactly what your four-week stay will involve, as every person’s experience with prescription stimulant dependency is deeply personal. At Gabriel Arcángel, we do not follow a pre-defined treatment path. Instead, we design a fully bespoke treatment plan that addresses your specific challenges. It is also important to emphasise that we do not adopt a rigid therapeutic model; we advocate a completely pragmatic approach to addiction, using whatever works best to help you achieve your goals.
Your days with us are carefully structured to include a variety of therapeutic elements designed to help you break the cycle of addiction and restore your well-being. This personalised combination of therapies addresses both physical dependence and the emotional, psychological, and behavioural aspects of addiction, including any mental health issues your use may have masked.
PrescriptionStimulants.section1Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we do not just treat symptoms; we identify and address the root causes of your prescription stimulant dependency. We begin with comprehensive diagnostics, as precise medical assessments are the foundation of effective treatment. Through functional and nutritional medicine assessments, we examine gut health, biochemical imbalances, heavy metal toxicity, parasites, and environmental toxins—factors that can influence addiction and mental health. This detailed analysis allows us to design a truly personalised, medically excellent treatment programme that restores your health holistically.
Your personalised treatment plan includes innovative therapies to address the root causes of your addiction while restoring your psychological, cellular, and biological well-being. To treat prescription stimulant addiction, we incorporate therapies such as Cognitive-Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT), sleep therapy, biofeedback, nutritional therapy, personal training, acupuncture, and art therapy.
However, we do not only address the causes of addiction but also its consequences, such as any neurological or physiological damage caused by substance use. These are treated in parallel with the underlying factors driving the addiction. Our treatments typically include intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, all aimed at reducing stress and supporting your recovery.
PrescriptionStimulants.section2Description:
We are the only residential prescription addiction treatment centre in the world to provide dedicated professionals to each client.
During your recovery, you will be supported by a team of professionals including doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists. They supervise your medically safe withdrawal and provide continuous care throughout your stay. A key member of this team is a live-in therapist, available to offer emotional support 24/7. No matter the unique blend of treatments you receive, all therapies are delivered with the Swiss medical excellence for which we are known.
PrescriptionStimulants.section3Description:
Guided by our core principles of empathy, innovation, and pragmatism, we design our intensive and holistic treatment programmes to suit you and your individual needs. These needs may be physical or psychological—for example, if our team identifies nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions during the assessment, your programme will be adjusted accordingly. They can also be practical needs, such as when a client needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
At our private residential drug treatment centre in Querétaro, we provide treatment for prescription stimulant addiction, as well as any co-occurring dependencies or mental health conditions that may be present.

PrescriptionDepressants.conditionHeading: Prescription Depressant Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
PrescriptionDepressants.conditionDescription: Trying to stop using depressant medications can feel impossible. Seeking professional support at a residential drug treatment centre gives you the best chance of recovery. Our personalized treatment programmes help you safely withdraw, separate your sense of identity from the addiction, and restore your overall health.
PrescriptionDepressants.whatIsDescription:
Prescription depressants, such as benzodiazepines (e.g., Valium, Xanax, and Ativan), barbiturates, and certain sleep medications, are commonly prescribed to manage anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms. While effective when used as directed, they carry a high risk of dependence and addiction.
Depressants work by slowing down the central nervous system, producing relaxation and sedation. Over time, the brain adapts to their effects, creating tolerance and requiring higher doses to achieve the same relief. This cycle increases the risk of dependency, withdrawal symptoms, and long-term mental health challenges such as depression, memory impairment, and emotional instability.
Misuse of prescription depressants can cause drowsiness, confusion, poor coordination, and, at high doses, dangerously slow breathing. Long-term abuse can alter brain function, leading to cognitive difficulties, mood disorders, and increased anxiety when not using the substance. Withdrawal from these medications can be particularly severe, including seizures, panic attacks, and rebound insomnia, making professional medical support essential for a safe recovery.
PrescriptionDepressants.signsTitle: What are the signs of prescription depressant addiction?
PrescriptionDepressants.signsDescription: As with other addictions, one of the most obvious signs of dependency is continuing to use the substance despite its negative impact on your life. Being unable to stop using depressants even when you want to is another key indicator.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of prescription depressant addiction
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom1Item1: Persistent sadness, anxiety, and mood swings.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom1Item2: Increasing the dosage to achieve the same calming or sedative effects.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom1Item3: Experiencing withdrawal symptoms, such as agitation, insomnia, or physical discomfort, when not taking depressants.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom1Item4: Withdrawing from social interactions and spending more time alone.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom1Item5: Avoiding situations or people where depressant use is not possible.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom1Item6: Neglecting personal hygiene, responsibilities, or once-enjoyed activities.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom1Item7: Persistent fatigue, drowsiness, and cognitive fog.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of prescription depressant addiction?
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom2Item1: Withdrawal symptoms: Severe muscle aches, sweating, nausea, vomiting, and tremors when not taking prescription depressants.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom2Item2: Dizziness and coordination issues: Frequent falls or trouble maintaining balance due to sedative effects.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom2Item3: Slurred speech: Noticeable difficulty speaking clearly, especially after taking depressants.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom2Item4: Chronic fatigue: Persistent drowsiness, lethargy, or lack of energy.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom2Item5: Slow heart rate and breathing: Depressants can dangerously slow down vital functions, increasing the risk of overdose.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptoms3Title: What are the emotional symptoms of prescription depressant addiction?
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom3Item1: Using prescription depressants to escape stress, anxiety, or emotional pain.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom3Item2: Intense guilt or shame about dependency and its impact on daily life.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom3Item3: Emotional numbness or detachment from reality while under the influence.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom3Item4: Frequent mood swings, including irritability or agitation when not using.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom3Item5: Withdrawing from social interactions and losing interest in relationships.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of prescription depressant addiction?
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom4Item1: Craving depressants to feel “normal” or avoid withdrawal symptoms.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom4Item3: Obsessing over obtaining and using depressants, often neglecting responsibilities.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom4Item4: Difficulty concentrating or making decisions due to cognitive fog.
PrescriptionDepressants.symptom4Item5: A growing sense of hopelessness or feeling trapped in dependency.
PrescriptionDepressants.dependenciesHeading: Types of prescription depressant addictions we treat
PrescriptionDepressants.dependenciesDescription: We understand unequivocally that pain is universal, but recovery has to be personal.
PrescriptionDepressants.howWeTreatHeading: How we treat Prescription Depressant Addiction
PrescriptionDepressants.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot tell you exactly what your four-week stay will involve, as each person’s experience with prescription depressant dependency is unique. At Gabriel Arcángel, we create fully bespoke treatment plans, ensuring your detox and recovery process is as safe, comfortable, and effective as possible. Our approach prioritizes harm reduction, supporting you to regain control of your well-being on your own terms.
Each day is carefully structured to integrate therapies tailored to your needs, addressing the physical, emotional, and psychological aspects of depressant dependency. Whether the focus is on managing withdrawal, building emotional resilience, or supporting long-term stability, our team guides you every step of the way.
PrescriptionDepressants.section1Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we do not just treat symptoms; we focus on sustainable recovery. Through comprehensive assessments, we design a holistic treatment programme that addresses the environmental, psychological, physical, and social root causes of your prescription depressant dependency.
Our approach prioritizes a safe, supervised detox, followed by a range of innovative therapies to address the causes of addiction while restoring your psychological, cellular, and biological well-being. To treat prescription depressant addiction, we incorporate therapies such as Cognitive-Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT), sleep therapy, biofeedback, nutritional therapy, massage therapy, acupuncture, and art therapy.
In addition to addressing the causes of addiction, we also treat its wider impacts, such as any neurological or physiological damage. These are addressed alongside the underlying factors driving your addiction. Our treatments typically include intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, all aimed at strengthening the mind-body connection, reducing stress, and supporting your recovery.
PrescriptionDepressants.section2Description:
We are the only residential treatment centre for prescription drug addictions in the world to provide dedicated professionals for each client.
During your recovery, you will be supported by a team of experts, including doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists. They supervise your safe withdrawal from medications and provide continuous care 24/7 throughout your stay. A key member of this team is a live-in therapist, available to provide constant emotional support. No matter the unique combination of therapies you receive, all will be delivered with the high level of medical excellence we are known for.
PrescriptionDepressants.section3Description:
Guided by our core principles of empathy, innovation, and pragmatism, we design intensive, comprehensive treatment programmes tailored to you and your needs. These needs may be physical or psychological; for example, if our team identifies nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions during the assessment, your programme will be adjusted accordingly. They can also be practical needs, such as when a client needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
At our private residential drug treatment centre in Querétaro, we provide treatment for prescription depressant addiction, as well as any co-occurring mental health conditions or dependencies.

PrescriptionOpioids.conditionHeading: Opioid Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
PrescriptionOpioids.conditionDescription: Trying to stop using opioids can feel almost impossible. Seeking professional support at a residential addiction treatment center gives you the best possible chance of recovery. Our specialized opioid treatment programs help you withdraw from the substance safely, separate your sense of identity from the addiction, and restore your physical and emotional health.
PrescriptionOpioids.whatIsDescription:
Opioids are a class of drugs derived from the opium poppy plant. They are highly potent sedatives primarily used for pain relief or anesthesia.
While some prescription opioids such as oxycodone (OxyContin), hydrocodone (Vicodin), codeine, and morphine are prescribed for pain management, illegal opioids such as heroin and fentanyl are often misused and carry significantly higher risks.
Prescription opioids, when used according to medical instructions, can help control pain, but they still carry the risk of dependency. Illegal opioids, on the other hand, are not regulated and can be far more dangerous, frequently leading to overdose and even death.
All opioids, whether prescribed or illicit, work by stimulating the release of endorphins in the brain. These chemicals block pain, increase feelings of pleasure, and create a temporary sense of well-being. This intense sensation can lead to cravings, and over time many people develop dependence, especially when the effects wear off.
In addition to strong physical dependence, opioids can also lead to psychological addiction. When a person uses opioids, the body reduces its natural production of endorphins. If someone is already struggling with low self-esteem, high stress levels, trauma, or other emotional difficulties, this lack of “feel-good chemicals” can trigger mental health conditions such as depression.
In turn, these mental health conditions can increase the likelihood of developing a substance dependence. Opioid abuse can also lead to tolerance, meaning that increasingly larger doses are required to achieve the same effect and release the needed endorphins. As the brain becomes more dependent on the substance and the doses increase, the risk of developing addiction grows significantly.
In the short term, opioids reduce the perception of pain and may cause drowsiness, confusion, and euphoria. At high doses, they can suppress breathing and cause an overdose that may stop respiration.
In the long term, opioid abuse alters the brain’s neurochemistry, which can lead to psychological addiction, tolerance, withdrawal symptoms, mood swings, and mental health disorders such as depression or psychosis.
PrescriptionOpioids.signsTitle: What are the signs of opioid addiction?
PrescriptionOpioids.signsDescription:
As with many other addictions, one of the most noticeable signs of opioid dependence is continuing to use the substance despite the negative consequences in personal, family, or professional life.
Another key sign is being unable to stop using opioids, even when the person genuinely wants to quit.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of opioid addiction
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom1Item2: Increasing the amount ingested to obtain the feeling of relief.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom1Item3: Experiencing withdrawal symptoms such as physical illness when not using opiods.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom1Item5: Avoiding places or people with whom you cannot consume opioids.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom1Item6: A lack of concern for personal hygiene, responsibilities or social activities.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of opioid addiction?
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom2Item1: Withdrawal symptoms: Severe muscle aches, sweating, nausea, vomiting, and diarrhoea when not using opioids.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom2Item2: Track marks: Visible scarring or sores from injecting opioids, like heroin or prescription painkillers.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom2Item3: Collapsed veins: A result of frequent intravenous use of opioids.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom2Item4: Pinpoint pupils: Noticeably smaller pupils, even in low light, a sign of opioid use.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom2Item5: Chronic fatigue: Constant exhaustion or low energy levels, often a result of opioid use.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom2Item6: Respiratory issues: Slow or laboured breathing, which can be life-threatening after opioid use.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptoms3Title: What are the emotional symptoms of opioid addiction?
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom3Item1: Using opioids as a way to escape feelings of sadness, anxiety, or emotional pain.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom3Item2: Experiencing intense shame or guilt about opioid use and the impact it has on life.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom3Item3: Emotional numbness or detachment from reality when under the influence of opioids.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom3Item4: Mood swings, including intense irritability or anger, especially when not using.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom3Item5: Emotional withdrawal from loved ones or meaningful relationships, feeling isolated.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of opioid addiction?
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom4Item1: Craving opioids to feel “normal” or avoid withdrawal symptoms.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom4Item2: Experiencing paranoia, confusion, or disorientation during or after use of opioids.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom4Item3: Obsessing over obtaining and using opioids, often at the expense of other priorities or responsibilities.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom4Item4: Difficulty concentrating, making decisions, or recalling memories while using or during withdrawal.
PrescriptionOpioids.symptom4Item5: Losing a sense of self-worth or hope for the future, feeling trapped in the cycle of addiction.
PrescriptionOpioids.dependenciesHeading: Types of opioid addictions we treat
PrescriptionOpioids.dependenciesDescription: We understand unequivocally that pain is universal, but recovery has to be personal.
PrescriptionOpioids.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot tell you exactly what your four-week stay will involve, because each patient’s experience with opioid addiction is deeply personal and unique. At our center, we recognize that recovery is different for every individual, which is why we create a personalized treatment plan for each person, without a predefined path.
Each day you spend with us is carefully structured and includes a variety of therapeutic elements specifically tailored to help you overcome opioid dependence and make the process as comfortable as possible.
These therapies are designed to complement one another and work together in an integrated way to restore your physical, emotional, and psychological health. Below, you will find more details.
PrescriptionOpioids.section1Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we do not treat symptoms alone. We carry out thorough evaluations to design a comprehensive treatment program that addresses the environmental, psychological, physical, and social causes of opioid dependence, with the goal of restoring the patient’s overall health.
This information allows us to create a fully personalized treatment program designed to help individuals understand the root causes of their addiction and how to overcome it.
For opioid dependence, we offer specialized therapies, including medication-assisted treatments such as Suboxone or methadone to help relieve withdrawal symptoms.
In addition, we integrate therapies such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), nutritional therapy, and advanced techniques such as biofeedback and mindfulness-based practices to support long-term recovery.
We focus not only on the causes of addiction but also on its effects, ranging from neurological changes to physical damage caused by substance use. These aspects are treated alongside the underlying factors that contribute to addiction.
Our approach integrates intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, all designed to reduce stress and promote lasting recovery.
PrescriptionOpioids.section2Description:
Your recovery process will be guided by a multidisciplinary team of dedicated professionals, including doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists.
Throughout your stay, you will receive continuous care, including medical supervision to ensure a safe detoxification process. In addition, you will have the support of a live-in therapist who will provide emotional support 24 hours a day.
Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all will be delivered with high medical standards and a professional approach focused on your well-being.
PrescriptionOpioids.section3Description:
Guided by our principles of empathy, innovation, and pragmatism, we design intensive and personalized treatment programs tailored to each individual’s needs.
These needs may be physical or psychological. For example, if during the evaluation our team identifies nutritional deficiencies or other addictions, the program will be adjusted to address them appropriately.
We also consider practical needs, such as when a patient needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
At our private residential treatment center in Querétaro, we provide specialized treatment for opioid addiction, as well as for other co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions.

Cannabis.conditionHeading: Cannabis Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
Cannabis.conditionDescription: We can help you break the cycle of needing cannabis to function, relax, or escape from the stress of daily life. Through our combination of medical treatments, psychological care, and personalized therapies, we address each of the underlying causes of cannabis addiction so you can regain control of your life.
Cannabis.whatIsHeading: How does someone become addicted to cannabis?
Cannabis.whatIsDescription:
Cannabis addiction is similar to alcohol addiction. Some people can use cannabis in moderation without experiencing problems, but for others this is not the case. For those who develop dependency, the impact can be significant both psychologically and physically.
Cannabis can become highly addictive for people who struggle with undiagnosed anxiety or depression. In many cases, it becomes addictive because it helps a person “disconnect from their own mind.” The psychoactive effects of cannabis create a sense of distance from the symptoms of anxiety or depression (which we often perceive as part of ourselves), producing a temporary feeling of relief.
It is precisely this relief — born from the need to escape those symptoms — that can make cannabis addictive.
Over time, the brain begins to associate calm or anxiety-free states with cannabis use, creating dependency and leading to tolerance. This means that a person needs to consume increasing amounts to achieve the same effect. Additionally, every time someone tries to quit, the underlying anxiety may return with greater intensity.
Cannabis use can also cause changes in cognitive function that affect learning, memory, and mood for days or even weeks. Long-term use may lead to more permanent impairments that increase anxiety levels, paranoia, and negatively affect personal relationships, professional life, and one’s relationship with oneself.
Paranoia is a very common side effect of chronic cannabis use and can intensify symptoms, especially when someone becomes trapped in the addictive cycle and feels compelled to use it despite knowing it may worsen feelings of paranoia.
In some cases, cannabis use may increase the likelihood of developing psychiatric conditions such as depression, anxiety, substance dependency, or even psychosis.
Cannabis.signsTitle: What are the signs of cannabis addiction?
Cannabis.signsDescription:
As with many other addictions, one of the most evident signs of cannabis dependency is continuing to use the substance despite the harm or distress it is causing in your life.
Another important sign is the inability to stop using cannabis, even when the person genuinely wants to quit.
Cannabis.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of cannabis addiction
Cannabis.symptom1Item1: Depending on cannabis to sleep, be creative, social, or relaxed.
Cannabis.symptom1Item2: Developing increased tolerance and experiencing withdrawal symptoms, such as insomnia, paranoia, or increased appetite.
Cannabis.symptom1Item3: Prioritising relationships, events, and activities based on whether or not you can smoke.
Cannabis.symptom1Item4: Continuing to use cannabis despite symptoms of paranoia, anxiety, or increased fear when high.
Cannabis.symptom1Item5: Using cannabis as a way to cope with sadness, boredom, anxiety, or other mental health challenges.
Cannabis.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of cannabis addiction?
Cannabis.symptom2Item2: Persistent cough or respiratory issues from smoking.
Cannabis.symptom2Item5: Reduced coordination or slower reaction times.
Cannabis.symptoms3Title: What are the emotional symptoms of cannabis addiction?
Cannabis.symptom3Item2: Feeling anxious, irritable, or moody when not using.
Cannabis.symptom3Item4: Emotional withdrawal from relationships or activities not associated with cannabis use.
Cannabis.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of cannabis addiction?
Cannabis.symptom4Item1: Using cannabis as a primary coping mechanism for stress or mental health struggles.
Cannabis.symptom4Item2: Paranoia or heightened anxiety, especially with higher doses.
Cannabis.symptom4Item4: Loss of interest in hobbies or activities previously enjoyed.
Cannabis.symptom4Item5: Rationalising or justifying use despite recognising its negative effects.
Cannabis.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot tell you exactly what your four-week stay will involve, as each person’s experience with cannabis dependency is unique. At our center, we understand that every individual’s relationship with cannabis is complex and multifaceted. For this reason, we design a fully personalized treatment plan for each patient, without a predefined path.
Your time with us is carefully structured to include a range of therapeutic elements specifically designed to help you free yourself from the hold cannabis has had on your life.
This personalized combination of therapies is designed to address every aspect of your physical, emotional, and psychological health, including any mental health conditions that cannabis use may be masking. Below you will find more details.
Cannabis.section1Description:
Gabriel Arcángel was one of the first residential addiction treatment centers to integrate functional medicine and interdisciplinary approaches into its treatment programs. From the beginning, we have understood that addictions are often the result of imbalances in different areas of life. True recovery requires understanding how and why those imbalances developed in order to treat the root causes, not just the symptoms.
For example, cannabis addiction often has emotional or psychological roots, such as using the substance to cope with chronic stress, anxiety, or past trauma. For some individuals, cannabis may provide temporary relief from overwhelming emotions or intrusive thoughts, which can lead to dependence on its calming effects.
On a physical level, the endocannabinoid system — which regulates mood, appetite, and sleep — can become dysregulated with frequent cannabis use, creating a cycle in which the body “craves” cannabis to restore balance.
In addition, cellular changes can occur, such as altered sensitivity of receptors in the brain, which reinforces the addictive cycle and makes it more difficult to stop using without experiencing withdrawal symptoms like irritability, insomnia, or increased anxiety.
For this reason, in addition to the detoxification process, your treatment program works to restore your well-being at the cellular, physical, and psychological levels through therapies such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), trauma-focused therapy, nutritional therapy, and acupuncture.
Alongside treating the root causes of cannabis addiction, we also address its long-term effects, including potential neurological or physical damage caused by substance use. Our approach works on these aspects while simultaneously addressing the deeper factors fueling the addiction.
Through intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, we focus on reducing stress and guiding you toward lasting recovery.
Cannabis.section2Description:
We are one of the few residential treatment centers that provides personalized professional care for each patient.
We offer continuous support to help you begin your recovery journey. In addition, you will have the support of a live-in therapist available 24 hours a day, from the moment you arrive until the end of your stay.
Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all will be delivered with high medical standards and the professional commitment that distinguishes us.
Cannabis.section3Description:
Guided by our principles of pragmatism and empathy, we strive to meet each patient exactly where they are and provide the treatment they truly need to recover.
At our residential addiction treatment center in Querétaro, we provide specialized care for cannabis addiction as well as for other co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions.

SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.pageHeading: Rehab for Synthetic Cathinones (Bath Salts) Addiction
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.conditionHeading: Bath Salts Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand how overwhelming it can be to face an addiction to bath salts. You do not have to go through this alone; we are here to help. Our treatment programs, built on a holistic and practical approach, are designed to support you through a safe detox process, address the root causes of addiction, and help restore your health.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.whatIsDescription:
Cathinones, commonly known as bath salts, are a dangerous synthetic drug with stimulant effects. This term does not refer to scented bath products used in the bathroom, but rather to a synthetic narcotic substance.
They are called bath salts because of their appearance: white or brown crystalline rocks or powder that resemble bath salts. In reality, they are not a single specific drug but a variety of chemically similar substances. In addition to being stimulants, they are classified as “new psychoactive substances” (NPS), about which scientific knowledge is still limited.
Bath salts became popular among teenagers and in nightclub environments in the early 2010s. However, their use has gradually declined as the harmful consequences have become more widely known.
The chemical composition of these substances is usually unknown, which means there is limited research on their physical and psychological effects. Depending on the combination of chemicals present, the effects may resemble those of cocaine, methamphetamine, or amphetamines. However, they are believed to be more than ten times stronger than cocaine.
Using bath salts can produce feelings of euphoria and intoxication, as well as increased sociability, heightened sex drive, and hallucinations. These effects typically last between three and four hours.
Due to the brain’s reward system — particularly the dopamine system — these substances have a strong potential for addiction. Over time, they can lead to severe physical and psychological dependence, along with various mental health issues.
When use continues over long periods, the brain adapts to the substance, causing the person to require increasingly higher doses to achieve the same effect. This phenomenon is known as tolerance.
Among the most dangerous side effects of bath salts are paranoia, suicidal tendencies, insomnia, and high blood pressure. The risk of overdose and even death can be high, as it is impossible to know exactly what chemicals each batch contains.
If you have developed an addiction to bath salts, recovery is possible. However, detoxing from these substances can be extremely difficult without professional support and a structured treatment program. In many cases, the safest way to do this is through a medical detox process that helps manage physical withdrawal symptoms, combined with the support of mental health professionals who address the psychological aspects of withdrawal.
Through our combination of medical care, psychological support, and personalized treatment, we address both co-occurring conditions and the many possible root causes of bath salts addiction, helping patients recover in a relatively short period of time.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.signsTitle: What are the signs of bath salts addiction?
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.signsDescription: Recognizing the signs of bath salts addiction is the first step toward seeking help. These substances can cause significant changes in behavior, mood, and physical health. Identifying the warning signs early can help prevent serious consequences and make it easier to begin appropriate treatment.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of bath salts addiction
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom1Item1: Continuing to use bath salts despite a wish to stop.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom1Item2: Needing to increase the dosage to achieve the desired effects.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom1Item3: Partaking in dangerous and risky behaviour to obtain the drug.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom1Item4: The development of withdrawal symptoms, such as fatigue, depression, irritability, restlessness, sleep disturbances, headaches, sweating and tremors when not taking the drug.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom1Item5: Isolating yourself from friends or loved ones.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of bath salts addiction?
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom2Item1: Rapid heart rate and potential cardiovascular complications.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom2Item2: Hyperactivity, fidgeting, or an inability to stay still.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom2Item3: Appetite suppression, leading to significant weight loss over time.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom2Item4: Fluctuations in body temperature such as sweating and chills.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom2Item5: Muscle tension or clenching, especially in the jaw or hands.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom2Item6: Skin sores caused by excessive scratching or picking due to hallucinations or delusions (e.g., feeling bugs under the skin).
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of bath salts addiction?
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom3Item1: An intense urge or compulsion to use bath salts, even when they cause harm.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom3Item2: Intense feelings of pleasure, euphoria, or invincibility during use.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom3Item3: Anxiety, paranoia, nervousness and irrational fears, even when not using bath salts.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom3Item4: Confusion, disorientation and a difficulty making sense of reality.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom3Item6: Mood swings: extreme changes in mood, from euphoria to depression.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom3Item7: Suicidal thoughts, especially during withdrawal or ‘crash’ periods.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom3Item8: Agitation, aggression, heightened irritability or violent outbursts.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of bath salts addiction?
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom4Item1: Using bath salts despite the negative consequences to your finances, career, and relationships.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom4Item2: Spending significant time and effort acquiring, using, or recovering from bath salts.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom4Item3: Seeking multiple sources for bath salts to maintain supply.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom4Item4: Risky behaviours: mixing bath salts with alcohol or other drugs, increasing the risk of overdose.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom4Item6: Neglecting work, school, or family responsibilities due to drug use.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.symptom4Item7: A loss of interest in relationships, activities and hobbies that once brought you joy.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.howWeTreatHeading: How we treat Bath Salts Addiction
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot say exactly what your four-week stay will involve, as our patients do not follow a predefined treatment path. Each person who comes to our center receives a fully personalized treatment plan designed around their specific needs.
From the withdrawal process to intensive therapies, every element of treatment is carefully tailored to the individual. It is important to emphasize that we do not follow a single treatment model. Instead, we apply a pragmatic approach to recovery, using the methods that work best for you to achieve your goals.
Each day of your stay is thoughtfully structured to include a variety of personalized therapies. These therapies complement one another and work together to address not only the physical aspects of addiction but also the emotional and psychological factors involved.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.section1Description:
We understand that every recovery journey is different, which is why we never use a “one-size-fits-all” approach. Instead, we design each program individually to adapt to your specific needs.
As part of recovery from bath salts addiction, we provide a carefully supervised detoxification process that may include medication-assisted treatment and intravenous therapies to help relieve withdrawal symptoms.
A key part of our approach is comprehensive clinical evaluation, which allows us to identify the underlying factors contributing to dependency. With this information, we design a personalized treatment plan that targets the root causes of addiction and supports long-term recovery.
Your program may include a wide range of therapies, including biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, trauma-focused therapy, acupuncture, equine therapy, and art therapy.
In addition to treating the causes of addiction, we also address its long-term effects, such as potential neurological or physical damage resulting from substance use. Our holistic approach combines intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, all aimed at reducing stress, promoting healing, and supporting a sustainable path to recovery.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.section2Description:
We have a multidisciplinary team made up of doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists who provide continuous care throughout the entire treatment process.
Our team offers ongoing support to supervise detoxification and guide you along the path to recovery. In addition, you will have access to emotional support from a live-in therapist available 24 hours a day.
Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all will be delivered with high medical standards and a professional approach focused on your well-being.
SyntheticCathinonesBathSalts.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design intensive and comprehensive treatment programs tailored to each patient. Guided by our principles of pragmatism and empathy, we develop therapeutic plans based on the unique needs of every individual.
These needs may be physical or psychological. For example, if during the evaluation we identify nutritional deficiencies or other addictions, the program will be adjusted to address these factors.
We also consider practical needs, such as when a patient needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
At our residential treatment center in Querétaro, we provide specialized care for bath salts addiction, as well as for other co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions.

SyntheticCannabinoids.pageHeading: Rehab for Synthetic Cannabinoids (Spice, K2) Addiction
SyntheticCannabinoids.conditionHeading: Synthetic Cannabinoids Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
SyntheticCannabinoids.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand how difficult it can be to overcome a spice addiction. You don’t have to face it alone; we’re here to help. Our treatment programs, built on a holistic and pragmatic approach, are designed to support you through a safe detox process, address the underlying causes of addiction, and help restore your health.
SyntheticCannabinoids.whatIsDescription:
Spice or K2 are street names used to refer to synthetic cannabinoids, also known as synthetic marijuana. This drug is an artificial version of marijuana that mimics its psychoactive effects but can be far more dangerous and unpredictable.
It generally consists of herbal mixtures that are sprayed with various chemicals and then sold under names such as “moon rocks” or “skunk.” It may produce feelings of relaxation and well-being, but higher doses can lead to confusion, rapid heart rate, and agitation.
This substitute for marijuana can sometimes be purchased legally in certain places without a prescription, which has made it popular among teenagers and young adults. However, despite its legal status in some contexts, it is not safe or harmless, and its use can lead to serious consequences.
In addition to mild side effects such as nausea, vomiting, or headaches, it can also cause more severe problems, including seizures, heart attacks, kidney failure, and even overdose.
Although the chemical composition of spice is often unknown, it is clear that it can be highly addictive. Over time, it can lead to strong physical and psychological dependence, as well as a variety of mental health issues.
When a person uses this substance over extended periods, the brain adapts to it, meaning increasingly higher doses are needed to achieve the same effect. This phenomenon is known as tolerance.
If you have developed a spice addiction, recovery is possible. However, detoxing from spice can be very challenging without professional support and structured treatment. In many cases, the safest approach involves a medical detox to help manage the physical withdrawal symptoms, along with the support of mental health specialists who can address the psychological aspects of recovery.
Through our combination of medical care, psychological support, and personalized attention, we address both co-occurring conditions and the potential root causes of spice addiction, helping patients recover in a relatively short period of time.
SyntheticCannabinoids.signsTitle: What are the signs of synthetic cannabinoids addiction?
SyntheticCannabinoids.signsDescription: Recognizing the signs of synthetic cannabinoid addiction is essential for seeking help in time. These substances can cause significant changes in behavior, mood, and physical health. Identifying the early warning signs allows for timely intervention, helping to prevent more serious consequences and making it easier to begin appropriate treatment.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of synthetic cannabinoids addiction
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom1Item1: Continuing to use spice despite a wish to stop.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom1Item2: Needing to increase the dosage to achieve the desired effects.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom1Item3: Partaking in dangerous and risky behaviour to obtain the drug.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom1Item4: The development of withdrawal symptoms, such as anxiety, depression, irritability, agitation, insomnia, nausea, vomiting, sweating, and tremors when not taking the drug.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom1Item5: Isolating yourself from friends or loved ones.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of synthetic cannabinoids addiction?
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom2Item1: Rapid heart rate and potential cardiovascular complications.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom2Item2: Severe drowsiness, fatigue or feeling ‘out of it’.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom2Item3: Nausea and vomiting associated with spice use.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom2Item5: Tremors, muscle spasms, uncontrolled shaking or twitching.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom2Item6: Dilated pupils or changes in eye appearance.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of synthetic cannabinoids addiction?
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom3Item1: An intense urge or compulsion to use spice, even when it causes you harm.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom3Item2: Euphoria or heightened pleasure during use.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom3Item3: Anxiety, paranoia and feeling fearful, or suspicious of others.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom3Item4: Confusion, disorientation, and a difficulty making sense of reality.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom3Item6: Agitation, aggression, heightened irritability, or violent outbursts.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom3Item7: Mood swings: extreme changes in mood, from euphoria to depression or anger.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of synthetic cannabinoids addiction?
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom4Item1: Using spice despite the negative consequences to your finances, career, and relationships.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom4Item2: Spending significant time and effort acquiring, using, or recovering from spice.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom4Item3: Seeking multiple sources for spice to maintain supply.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom4Item4: Risky behaviours: mixing spice with alcohol or other drugs, increasing the risk of overdose.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom4Item6: Neglecting work, school, or family responsibilities due to drug use.
SyntheticCannabinoids.symptom4Item7: A loss of interest in relationships, activities and hobbies that once brought you joy.
SyntheticCannabinoids.howWeTreatHeading: How we treat Synthetic Cannabinoids Addiction
SyntheticCannabinoids.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot say exactly what your four-week stay will involve because every person’s recovery journey is unique. For this reason, we design a fully personalized treatment plan for each patient who comes to our center, with no predefined treatment path.
Likewise, we do not follow a single treatment model. Instead, we take a pragmatic approach to recovery, using the strategies that work best to help you achieve your personal goals.
Each day of your stay is carefully structured to include a range of therapeutic elements specifically designed to support your recovery. From managing withdrawal symptoms to intensive therapy, every stage of treatment is tailored to your needs.
These therapies work together in a complementary way to address not only the physical aspects of addiction but also the emotional and psychological factors involved.
SyntheticCannabinoids.section1Description:
We understand that every recovery journey is different, which is why we never use a “one-size-fits-all” approach. Instead, we carefully adapt each program to meet your specific needs.
As part of treatment for spice addiction, we offer a supervised detoxification process that may include medication-assisted treatment and intravenous therapies to help ease withdrawal symptoms.
A key component of our approach is a comprehensive clinical evaluation, which helps us identify the underlying factors contributing to dependency. With this information, we create a personalized treatment plan that addresses the root causes of addiction and supports long-term recovery.
Your program may include a wide range of therapies, such as biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, trauma-focused therapy, acupuncture, equine-assisted therapy, and art therapy.
In addition to addressing the causes of addiction, we also focus on the broader effects of spice abuse, such as potential neurological or physical damage. Our holistic approach combines intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies to reduce stress, promote healing, and support a sustainable path to recovery.
SyntheticCannabinoids.section2Description:
We have a multidisciplinary team made up of doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists who provide continuous care throughout the treatment process.
Our team offers ongoing support to monitor the withdrawal process and guide you on your path toward recovery. In addition, you will have access to emotional support from a live-in therapist available 24 hours a day.
Regardless of the specific combination of treatments you receive, they will all be delivered with high medical standards and a professional approach focused on your well-being.
SyntheticCannabinoids.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design comprehensive and intensive treatment programs tailored to each individual. Guided by our principles of pragmatism and empathy, we develop therapeutic plans based on each person’s unique needs.
These needs may be physical or psychological. For example, if during the assessment our team identifies nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions, the program will be adjusted to address these factors.
We also consider practical needs, such as when a patient must work or travel during their stay with us.
At our residential addiction treatment center in Querétaro, we provide specialized care for spice addiction, as well as for other dependencies and mental health conditions that may occur at the same time.

Kratom.conditionHeading: Kratom Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
Kratom.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we are here to help you break free from kratom addiction. Our personalised treatment programmes guide you through a safe detoxification process while addressing the underlying causes that may be driving the addiction. Because individuals can develop dependence on both the stimulant effects and the opioid-like effects of this substance, a comprehensive and integrative approach like ours is essential for achieving a full recovery.
Kratom.whatIsDescription:
Kratom is a psychotropic substance (one that alters mental state) derived from the leaves of a Southeast Asian plant known as Mitragyna speciosa. Although kratom has been used as a legal aid for opioid withdrawal since 1836, its use has increased significantly in recent years.
For hundreds of years, kratom has also been used as a natural remedy to treat depression and relieve pain. However, while it may help some individuals, for many others it creates more problems than it solves.
A concerning issue is that because kratom is not yet illegal in many places, many users are unaware of the risks associated with its misuse. As an unregulated substance with addictive potential, more and more people are replacing an opioid dependency with a kratom dependency.
Kratom is addictive because it contains mitragynine, a compound that can be approximately 13 times more potent than morphine. At low doses, it interacts with brain receptors to produce a stimulant effect. However, at higher doses, mitragynine and another kratom compound known as 7-alpha-hydroxymitragynine interact with opioid receptors in the brain.
As a result, these compounds produce effects very similar to opioids, such as increased feelings of pleasure and reduced pain. This also means that kratom can create a dependency comparable to both opioids and stimulants. Some experts even argue that this combination of addictive properties may make kratom more addictive than substances such as cocaine or heroin.
Additionally, kratom contains several substances that remain poorly studied. For example, some experts have observed that one of its compounds may act similarly to benzodiazepines, medications commonly used to treat anxiety.
Another important factor is that kratom produces both stimulant and opioid-like effects. This means that a person may develop a dual dependency, becoming addicted to both the stimulating and the sedating effects on the brain. This can make treatment more complex, though it is certainly not impossible.
Kratom abuse can lead to many side effects, including depression, anxiety, hostility, irritability, insomnia, muscle pain, coma, heart attacks, high blood pressure, abnormal heart rhythms, psychosis, hallucinations, and opioid-like withdrawal symptoms. The risk of overdose increases significantly when kratom is combined with other substances.
A kratom addiction can become overwhelming, especially when a person begins using it in the hope of stopping other opioid use. It can be discouraging to realise that one is still trapped in a cycle of addiction, and overcoming that cycle alone can be extremely difficult.
In many cases, the only effective way to overcome kratom addiction is through medical detoxification, which helps manage the physical withdrawal symptoms, combined with the support of mental health professionals who address the psychological aspects of dependence.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personal care, we focus on addressing co-occurring conditions and the possible root causes of kratom addiction. In doing so, we help individuals move forward in their recovery in a shorter and more structured timeframe.
Kratom.signsTitle: What are the signs of kratom addiction?
Kratom.signsDescription: The signs of kratom addiction can appear on physical, emotional, and behavioural levels. Over time, frequent use can lead to dependency, mood changes, and difficulty controlling consumption. Recognising these signs early is essential to seeking professional support and preventing the addiction from progressing. Identifying early symptoms can make a significant difference in the recovery process.
Kratom.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of kratom addiction
Kratom.symptom1Item1: Continuing to use kratom despite a wish to stop.
Kratom.symptom1Item2: Needing to increase the dosage to achieve the desired effects.
Kratom.symptom1Item3: Partaking in dangerous and risky behaviour to obtain the drug.
Kratom.symptom1Item4: You started taking kratom to help opioid withdrawal, but now you are taking both kratom and opioids, or your kratom abuse is now mirroring how you used to abuse opioids.
Kratom.symptom1Item5: The development of withdrawal symptoms, such as irritability, agitation, insomnia, sweating, chills, nausea, vomiting, muscle pains and tremors when not taking the drug.
Kratom.symptom1Item6: Isolating yourself from friends or loved ones.
Kratom.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of kratom addiction?
Kratom.symptom2Item2: Feeling sluggish and lethargic, especially after heavy use or during withdrawal.
Kratom.symptom2Item3: Reduced appetite leading to weight loss or, in some cases, binge eating during withdrawal.
Kratom.symptom2Item4: Muscle aches or pain, especially when attempting to reduce or stop kratom use.
Kratom.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of kratom addiction?
Kratom.symptom3Item1: An intense urge or compulsion to use kratom, even when it causes harm.
Kratom.symptom3Item2: Feeling unable to function or cope with stress without kratom.
Kratom.symptom3Item3: Mood swings: fluctuations between irritability, anxiety, and euphoria.
Kratom.symptom3Item4: Anxiety or depression, particularly during withdrawal periods.
Kratom.symptom3Item5: Cognitive impairment such as difficulty concentrating, memory problems, or confusion.
Kratom.symptom3Item6: Paranoia, agitation, restlessness or irrational fears, especially at high doses.
Kratom.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of kratom addiction?
Kratom.symptom4Item1: Using kratom despite the negative consequences to your finances, career, and relationships.
Kratom.symptom4Item2: Seeking multiple sources for kratom to maintain supply.
Kratom.symptom4Item3: Risky behaviours: mixing kratom with alcohol or other drugs, increasing the risk of overdose.
Kratom.symptom4Item5: Neglecting work, school, or family responsibilities due to drug use.
Kratom.symptom4Item6: A loss of interest in relationships, activities and hobbies that once brought you joy.
Kratom.howWeTreatDescription:
