We cannot say exactly what your four-week stay will look like, as each person’s experience with kratom addiction is deeply personal. For this reason, we design a fully individualised treatment plan for every person who receives care at our clinic.
There is no fixed treatment path, nor do we rely on a single therapeutic model. Instead, we promote a practical and flexible approach to recovery, focusing on what works best to help you achieve your goals.
Each day is carefully structured and includes a combination of therapeutic interventions designed to help you overcome kratom addiction and develop healthier coping mechanisms.
Every stage of the process is tailored to you, from withdrawal management to intensive therapy. All the therapies we use complement each other in order to support recovery by restoring your physical, emotional, and psychological health.
Kratom.section1Description:
We understand that every recovery journey is different, which is why we never adopt a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we carefully tailor each programme to meet the specific needs of every individual.
As part of recovery from kratom addiction, we offer a medically supervised withdrawal process using Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT) and IV therapy to help ease withdrawal symptoms.
Another key component of our approach is comprehensive testing and assessments, which allow us to identify the underlying causes of dependency. Based on this information, we develop a personalised treatment plan that directly addresses these root causes and supports long-term recovery. Your personalised programme may include a variety of therapies such as: Biofeedback, Nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), Sleep therapies, Personalised physical training, Acupuncture, Equine-assisted therapy, Art therapy.
In addition to addressing the root causes of addiction, we also focus on any neurological or physical effects that may have resulted from kratom use.
Our holistic approach combines intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, working together to reduce stress, promote healing, and support sustainable recovery.
Kratom.section2Description:
Our team is composed of doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists who work collaboratively to provide comprehensive care.
The team provides continuous support to guide you through the withdrawal process and help you move forward in your recovery. You will also receive therapeutic support to ensure emotional care throughout the entire process.
Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all care is delivered with high professional standards and a strong focus on your wellbeing.
Kratom.section3Description:
At our clinic, we design comprehensive and intensive treatment programmes tailored to each individual. Guided by principles of empathy, understanding, and personalised care, we develop a treatment plan based on your specific needs.
These needs may be physical or psychological. For example, if nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions are identified during the initial assessment, the programme will be adjusted to address these conditions.
We also consider practical factors, such as personal or professional responsibilities that patients may have during their recovery process.
We provide treatment for kratom addiction, as well as other dependencies and related mental health conditions, within a professional, discreet environment focused on comprehensive recovery.

Ketamine.conditionHeading: Ketamine Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
Ketamine.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we are here to support you in overcoming ketamine addiction, no matter how difficult it may seem. Our approach is compassionate, practical, and free of judgment. We will provide you with the tools needed to safely withdraw from ketamine, manage withdrawal symptoms, and explore the underlying causes of your addiction, helping you move forward with greater strength and clarity.
Ketamine.whatIsDescription:
Ketamine is a dissociative anesthetic primarily used in veterinary hospitals as a tranquilizer. It is also used in certain medical procedures as an anesthetic. However, it is also obtained illegally and used recreationally for its hallucinogenic effects.
Dissociative drugs are hallucinogens that can cause a person to feel detached from reality. Ketamine’s popularity stems from its mood-altering properties and the trance-like state it can induce, although these effects are typically short-lived.
Ketamine is a powerful substance with a complex nature. When used as a medication under professional supervision, it can be extremely beneficial. However, it also carries a risk of addiction when misused. While it can offer significant therapeutic benefits, its ability to stimulate the release of dopamine in the brain creates a sense of euphoria that, over time, can lead to dependence.
At high doses, it can even trigger an experience known as a “K-hole,” which some people describe as a sensation of being detached from the body or similar to a near-death experience.
At Gabriel Arcángel, we offer a combination of medical, psychological, and personal care. Our approach addresses not only addiction itself but also co-occurring conditions and the underlying causes of ketamine dependence. Through a personalised treatment programme, we help individuals progress in their recovery in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
Ketamine.signsTitle: What are the signs of ketamine addiction?
Ketamine.signsDescription: Prolonged ketamine abuse can lead to chemical changes in the brain that make it very difficult to stop using without professional help. The symptoms of ketamine addiction are similar to those seen in other substance use disorders.
Ketamine.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of ketamine addiction
Ketamine.symptom1Item1: Being unable to stop using ketamine despite wanting to quit.
Ketamine.symptom1Item2: Becoming tolerant to ketamine, meaning you need increasingly larger doses of ketamine to achieve the same high.
Ketamine.symptom1Item3: Feeling you need the drug to feel “normal”.
Ketamine.symptom1Item4: Feeling preoccupied with the substance to the detriment of one’s relationships, career or well-being.
Ketamine.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of ketamine addiction?
Ketamine.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of ketamine addiction?
Ketamine.symptom3Item3: Cognitive difficulties even when not using the drug.
Ketamine.symptom3Item4: Increasing sense that you only feel yourself when you are using ketamine.
Ketamine.symptom3Item5: Increasing worry or fear that your ketamine use has become a problem.
Ketamine.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of ketamine addiction?
Ketamine.symptom4Item1: Feeling as though ketamine has taken over your life.
Ketamine.symptom4Item3: Only socialising with those who take ketamine.
Ketamine.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot say exactly what your four-week stay will involve, as each person’s journey with ketamine addiction is unique. At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that no two recovery paths are the same, which is why each individual requires a completely personalised treatment plan tailored to their specific needs.
It is important to emphasise that we do not follow a single treatment model. Instead, we promote a practical and flexible approach to recovery, focused on what works best to help you achieve your goals.
Your stay with us will be carefully structured and will include a variety of therapies specifically selected to address your ketamine dependency. We also provide supportive treatments, such as intravenous infusions (IVs), along with other specialised therapies to ensure the process is as comfortable as possible.
Each therapy complements the others, aiming to treat not only the physical aspects of addiction but also the emotional and psychological factors involved.
Ketamine.section1Description:
Gabriel Arcángel was one of the first residential addiction treatment centres in Querétaro to introduce functional medicine and interdisciplinary modalities into its treatment programmes.
From the beginning, we have understood that addictions are often the result of imbalances in different areas of life. True recovery requires understanding how and why these imbalances developed so that we can treat the underlying causes, not just the symptoms.
If necessary, we will guide you through a safe, medically supervised withdrawal process, overseen by our psychiatrist and supported 24/7 by our clinical team.
After the detox process, we will carry out comprehensive evaluations and testing to understand the specific factors contributing to your ketamine dependency. Based on this information, we will develop a treatment plan that includes intensive psychotherapy to address the psychological effects of ketamine use, along with biochemical restoration to help rebalance your body.
We also incorporate complementary therapies designed to support your physical and mental recovery, helping you regain control and restore your overall wellbeing. To treat ketamine addiction, we draw on a variety of therapies, including: Biofeedback, Nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behaviour Therapy (DBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), Sleep therapies, Acupuncture, Art therapy.
Our approach focuses not only on addressing the root causes of addiction but also on treating its broader effects, including any neurological or physical damage caused by ketamine use.
These factors are treated alongside the underlying causes of addiction with the goal of reducing stress and supporting a strong, sustainable recovery.
Ketamine.section2Description:
We provide personalised care and professional support throughout the entire recovery process.
Our team offers 24-hour support to help guide you on your path to recovery. In addition, therapeutic support will be available at all times to provide emotional assistance from the moment you arrive until the completion of your treatment.
Regardless of the combination of therapies you receive, all treatments are delivered with high professional standards and a strong focus on your wellbeing.
Ketamine.section3Description:
We design comprehensive and intensive treatment programmes tailored to each individual’s specific needs. Guided by our core principles of empathy, understanding, and a practical approach, we develop a treatment plan based on your personal situation, realistic possibilities, goals, and motivation for change.
We provide treatment for ketamine addiction, including the misuse of medical or prescribed ketamine, as well as other co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, within a professional and discreet environment focused on comprehensive recovery in Querétaro.

Nicotine.conditionHeading: Nicotine Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
Nicotine.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we do not adopt a one-size-fits-all approach to treating nicotine addiction. While we provide a medically supervised detoxification period and help you find strategies to manage withdrawal symptoms, our priority is to address the underlying causes of your addiction through an approach based on understanding and pragmatism.
Nicotine.whatIsDescription:
Nicotine is a highly addictive stimulant found in cigarettes, vapes, chewing tobacco, and other tobacco products. Nicotine addiction, also known as tobacco use disorder, occurs when a person becomes dependent on this substance.
This addiction is both physical, due to nicotine’s effects on the brain, and psychological, related to habits, routines, and cravings. As a result, quitting can be extremely difficult, even when someone is fully aware of the health risks.
What makes nicotine so addictive is the way it affects the brain’s reward system. Similar to other addictive substances such as caffeine, alcohol, or certain drugs, nicotine stimulates the release of dopamine, a brain chemical that creates a temporary feeling of pleasure and relaxation.
However, once that effect fades, cravings return, leading to a cycle of dependence that reinforces the habit. Over time, the brain adapts to nicotine and requires higher doses to achieve the same effects, a phenomenon known as tolerance.
When the body begins to rely on nicotine to feel “normal,” and withdrawal symptoms appear when nicotine levels drop, a dependency has developed. The habit of smoking or vaping becomes even more reinforced when it is linked to daily routines, emotions, or social environments, making quitting even more difficult.
There are various emotional, physical, and social factors that may contribute to the development of nicotine dependence. For example, some individuals may have a genetic predisposition or may be more likely to develop addiction if close family members smoke or vape.
Early exposure to nicotine is a significant risk factor. People who begin using nicotine during adolescence are far more likely to develop long-term addiction because the adolescent brain is more sensitive to its effects.
Psychological factors also play a role. Many people smoke or vape as a way to cope with stress, anxiety, depression, or boredom. In addition, environmental influences such as peer pressure, the normalization of smoking or vaping, and the portrayal of smoking as “attractive” in media can also contribute to the initiation of use.
Nicotine has a serious impact on physical health. Long-term use increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, high blood pressure, and cancer. Even vaping, which is often considered “safer,” exposes the body to potentially harmful chemicals.
Nicotine addiction can also worsen mental health issues. Many people smoke or vape to calm their nerves, but nicotine can actually increase stress hormones such as cortisol.
In any case, the relaxing effect is only temporary. Once nicotine leaves the body, anxiety may return with greater intensity, reinforcing the cycle of dependence.
Nicotine addiction is also associated with low mood and depression, due to the way it affects dopamine production. People may struggle to experience pleasure without smoking or vaping. Over time, the brain becomes less responsive to natural rewards such as food, hobbies, or social interaction and begins to seek stronger dopamine stimulation.
Because nicotine is a stimulant, it can also lead to insomnia, restless sleep, and fatigue due to poor sleep quality. In addition, having a nicotine addiction may increase the risk of developing dependence on other substances such as alcohol, caffeine, or drugs.
Although some individuals manage to overcome nicotine addiction on their own, many others struggle to quit despite their best efforts. Professional support significantly increases the chances of successfully quitting.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personal care, we focus on addressing co-occurring conditions and the possible root causes of nicotine addiction. In doing so, we help individuals move forward in their recovery in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
Nicotine.signsTitle: What are the signs of nicotine addiction?
Nicotine.signsDescription: If your nicotine dependency is negatively affecting your life, your health, or your relationships, it is possible that you have developed an addiction and may require treatment.
Nicotine.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of nicotine addiction
Nicotine.symptom1Item1: Feeling a lack of control over your nicotine use.
Nicotine.symptom1Item3: Intense cravings for nicotine, often to the point where you will use the drug despite its consequences on your relationships, career, and life.
Nicotine.symptom1Item4: Withdrawal symptoms such as irritability, mood swings, anxiety, restlessness, difficulty concentrating, headaches, increased appetite, weight gain, and sleep disturbances such as insomnia or vivid dreams.
Nicotine.symptom1Item5: A growing sense that you are losing connection to who you really are.
Nicotine.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of nicotine addiction?
Nicotine.symptom2Item1: Strong cravings for nicotine (cigarettes, vapes, chewing tobacco, etc.).
Nicotine.symptom2Item2: Increased heart rate and blood pressure after nicotine use.
Nicotine.symptom2Item3: Shortness of breath, especially in long-term smokers or vapers.
Nicotine.symptom2Item4: Chronic cough, often called a “smoker’s cough”.
Nicotine.symptom2Item5: Yellowing of fingers and nails from tar in cigarettes.
Nicotine.symptom2Item6: Dry skin, premature aging, and wrinkles due to reduced blood flow.
Nicotine.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of nicotine addiction?
Nicotine.symptom3Item1: Using nicotine to relieve stress or anxiety.
Nicotine.symptom3Item2: Feeling unable to function without nicotine.
Nicotine.symptom3Item3: Loss of control over use, even when trying to quit.
Nicotine.symptom3Item4: Strong emotional attachment to smoking/vaping habits.
Nicotine.symptom3Item5: Experiencing depression or mood swings when trying to quit.
Nicotine.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of nicotine addiction?
Nicotine.symptom4Item1: Using nicotine in larger amounts or for longer periods than intended.
Nicotine.symptom4Item2: Spending significant time and money on tobacco or nicotine products.
Nicotine.symptom4Item3: Avoiding places where smoking/vaping is not allowed.
Nicotine.symptom4Item4: Continuing to smoke/vape despite knowing the health risks.
Nicotine.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot say exactly what your four-week stay will involve, as each person’s experience with nicotine dependency is deeply personal.
At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand the complexity of each individual’s relationship with nicotine. For this reason, we do not follow a predefined treatment path. Instead, we design a personalised treatment plan for each person, focused on their specific challenges and needs.
Each day with us is carefully structured to include a variety of therapeutic interventions designed to help you break the cycle of addiction and restore your well-being.
This personalised combination of therapies is designed to address both the physical dependence and the emotional, psychological, and behavioural aspects of addiction. This also includes treating any mental health conditions that nicotine use may be masking or worsening.
Nicotine.section1Description:
We do not use a standardised approach for everyone. Instead, we adapt each programme to the specific needs of each individual.
Alongside a carefully supervised withdrawal process, a central part of our approach is conducting comprehensive evaluations and testing to identify all possible causes of your nicotine dependency.
This information allows us to design a treatment programme that helps you understand why you developed the addiction and what steps you can take to overcome it.
Your personalised treatment plan may include advanced therapies aimed at addressing the root causes of addiction while restoring your psychological, cellular, and biological well-being.
To treat nicotine addiction, we incorporate therapies such as: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT), Hypnotherapy, Biofeedback, Nutritional therapy, Personalised physical training, Acupuncture, Art therapy.
In addition to treating the root causes of addiction, we also address its potential long-term effects, such as neurological or physical damage associated with substance use.
Our treatments typically combine intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, all aimed at reducing stress, developing healthier coping mechanisms, and supporting long-term recovery.
Nicotine.section2Description:
We provide personalised care and professional support throughout the entire recovery process.
Your treatment will be guided by a team of doctors, therapists, and specialists who provide continuous support. This includes supervision of a medically safe withdrawal process when necessary.
In addition, you will have ongoing therapeutic support to provide emotional assistance 24 hours a day throughout your recovery.
Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all care is delivered with high professional standards and a strong focus on your wellbeing.
Nicotine.section3Description:
Each of our treatment programmes is carefully designed according to the specific needs of each individual.
These needs may be physical, psychological, or practical. For example, if nutritional deficiencies or signs of other dependencies are identified during the initial assessment, the programme will be adapted to address these factors.
We also consider practical aspects, such as when a patient needs to continue with work responsibilities or travel during their treatment.
Although the exact treatments may vary, one constant across all our programmes is the continuous support of our team of specialists.
We provide treatment for nicotine addiction, as well as for other co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, within our residential addiction treatment centre in Querétaro.

OxycodoneOxycontin.conditionHeading: OxyContin Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
OxycodoneOxycontin.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we are here to help you overcome your OxyContin addiction, no matter how overwhelming it may feel. In a private and comfortable setting, we guide you through a safe, specialist-supervised detox process while addressing the root causes of addiction and supporting the full restoration of your health.
OxycodoneOxycontin.whatIsDescription:
OxyContin is an opioid that belongs to the group of narcotic analgesics. This powerful pain medication works by reducing the perception of pain, creating a sense of detachment. It is commonly prescribed for severe pain resulting from major surgery, cancer, or when other painkillers have not been effective. However, OxyContin can be highly addictive. What often begins as legitimate medical use of oxycodone can quickly spiral out of control, as tolerance to this powerful opioid can increase rapidly.
Physical dependence occurs when an individual comes to rely on OxyContin to a significant degree. The body becomes so accustomed to the substance that, in its absence, it feels as though something essential is missing, leading to withdrawal symptoms.
Psychological dependence develops when a person uses OxyContin to numb or escape emotional pain, such as stress, loneliness, or traumatic experiences. In most cases, individuals struggling with prescription drug addiction experience a combination of both physical and psychological dependence.
At Gabriel Arcángel, we have treated many patients with OxyContin addiction, partly as a result of the opioid crisis that began in the 1990s and resurged in the early 2010s. During this crisis, there was a significant rise in overuse, abuse, and overdose-related deaths involving opioids, with OxyContin being one of the main substances involved due to overprescription.
The risk of overdose and death is especially high during relapse. When someone stops using and later relapses, their tolerance is lower, which greatly increases the risk of overdose. Although the situation has improved in some countries due to better medical practices, oxycodone remains highly addictive.
Over time, OxyContin abuse can seriously impact health. It increases the risk of respiratory depression, liver and kidney damage, seizures, and the development of other mental health disorders or substance use issues. In fact, some studies suggest that a significant percentage of people who use heroin began with an addiction to prescription opioids.
If you have developed an OxyContin addiction, it’s important to know that recovery is possible. However, due to its highly addictive nature, detoxing can be very difficult without professional support and structured treatment. In many cases, the safest approach is a medically supervised detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, along with support from mental health professionals to address the psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical care, psychological support, and personalized treatment, we address both co-occurring conditions and the underlying causes of OxyContin addiction, helping patients recover in a relatively short period of time.
OxycodoneOxycontin.signsTitle: What are the signs of OxyContin addiction?
OxycodoneOxycontin.signsDescription: Recognizing the signs of OxyContin addiction is essential to taking action in time. This type of dependency can manifest through physical, emotional, and behavioral changes. Detecting these signs early helps prevent serious consequences and encourages seeking professional support. Identifying the problem is the first step toward recovery.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of OxyContin addiction
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom1Item1: Wanting to cut down or stop using OxyContin but feeling unable to.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom1Item2: Ingesting OxyContin even though your pain has subsided.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom1Item3: Lying or exaggerating pain to get prescribed OxyContin.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom1Item4: Taking OxyContin in response to stress, anxiety or boredom.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom1Item5: Needing higher doses of OxyContin to achieve the same effect.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom1Item7: Withdrawal symptoms when not taking the drug, such as: nausea and vomiting, diarrhea, sweating, chills, muscle aches, cramps, insomnia, restlessness, or agitation.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of OxyContin addiction?
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom2Item1: Feeling physically ill or uncomfortable without the drug.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom2Item2: Changes in appearance such as fatigue and weight loss, and neglect of personal hygiene.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of OxyContin addiction?
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom3Item1: Intense desire or obsession to take oxycodone.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom3Item2: Mood changes such as anxiety, depression, or irritability, especially when you’re unable to access the drug.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of OxyContin addiction?
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom4Item1: Using OxyContin despite the negative consequences to your finances, career and relationships.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom4Item2: Visiting multiple doctors or chemists to obtain prescriptions.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom4Item3: Withdrawing from friends, family, and social activities.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom4Item4: Neglecting responsibilities at work, school, or home.
OxycodoneOxycontin.symptom4Item5: Engaging in risky behaviors such as driving under the influence or obtaining OxyContin illegally.
OxycodoneOxycontin.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot tell you exactly what your four-week stay will involve, as each person’s recovery journey is unique. At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that every patient requires a fully personalized treatment plan tailored to their specific needs.
It is important to emphasize that we do not follow a single treatment model. Instead, we take a pragmatic approach, using the strategies that work best to help you achieve your goals. This includes, when necessary, the use of medication-assisted treatment (MAT), without requiring complete abstinence from other substances such as nicotine or alcohol.
Your stay with us will be carefully structured to include a variety of therapies specifically selected to treat OxyContin dependency and help you regain control of your life. When needed, this includes a medically supervised detox process supported by intravenous therapies and complementary treatments to ease withdrawal symptoms.
All therapies work together in an integrated way to address not only the physical aspects of addiction but also the emotional and psychological factors involved.
OxycodoneOxycontin.section1Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that every recovery journey is different, which is why we never use a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we carefully design each program to fit your individual needs.
Our comprehensive approach goes beyond helping you through withdrawal and managing symptoms. A key part of the process involves conducting in-depth assessments to identify the root causes of OxyContin dependency. With this insight, we develop a personalized treatment plan that helps you understand the origin of your addiction and how to overcome it.
Your program may include a variety of therapies such as biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), bioresonance, Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, acupuncture, and equine-assisted therapy.
In addition to addressing the root causes of addiction, we also focus on its effects, such as potential neurological or physical damage. Our multidisciplinary approach combines intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, all aimed at reducing stress and supporting long-term recovery.
OxycodoneOxycontin.section2Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we have a multidisciplinary team of professionals dedicated to each patient.
Your treatment will be guided by a team of doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists who will supervise your detox process and provide continuous care with high medical standards. In addition, you will have the support of a live-in therapist available 24 hours a day to offer emotional support.
Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all will be delivered with a professional approach focused on your well-being.
OxycodoneOxycontin.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design comprehensive and intensive treatment programs tailored to the specific needs of each patient. Guided by our principles of empathy and pragmatism, we create fully personalized therapeutic plans.
These needs may be physical or psychological. For example, if nutritional deficiencies or other addictions are identified during the assessment, your program will be adjusted to address these factors.
We also take practical needs into account, such as when a patient needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
At our residential treatment center in Querétaro, we provide specialized care for OxyContin addiction, as well as for other co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions.

BuprenorphineSuboxone.conditionHeading: Suboxone Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
BuprenorphineSuboxone.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we can help you overcome your Suboxone addiction, no matter how difficult it may seem. Our support goes beyond ensuring a safe, expert-supervised detox and managing your symptoms; we work to address the underlying causes of your addiction and restore your health.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.whatIsDescription:
Suboxone is an opioid that belongs to the narcotic analgesics group of medications. This powerful pain reliever works by reducing the perception of pain. It is most commonly prescribed for moderate to severe pain caused by major surgeries, injuries, cancer, or when other painkillers have not been effective. It is also used to treat drug dependence, for example during withdrawal from opioids such as heroin.
Suboxone is not extremely addictive on its own, as it does not produce a significant “high.” The main challenge it presents is the fear of the withdrawal process, which can be particularly difficult. That said, it can still be addictive. Physical dependence occurs when a person relies on Suboxone excessively. The body becomes so accustomed to the substance that, in its absence, it feels something essential is missing. As a result, withdrawal symptoms appear.
Psychological dependence arises when we begin to use Suboxone to avoid or numb emotional pain, such as stress, loneliness, or trauma. Most individuals with prescription medication addiction experience varying levels of both physical and psychological dependence.
Over time, Suboxone abuse can cause serious health damage. It increases the risk of respiratory depression, liver and kidney damage, seizures, and the development of other mental health disorders or substance use issues.
If you have developed a Suboxone addiction, it is not your fault, and there is a way to overcome it. However, detoxing from buprenorphine without professional, structured support can be very challenging. In many cases, the best option is to undergo a medical detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, along with the support of mental health specialists to address the psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personal care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that addresses both co-occurring conditions and the root causes of Suboxone addiction. In doing so, we can help you recover in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.signsTitle: What are the signs of Suboxone addiction?
BuprenorphineSuboxone.signsDescription: Identifying the signs of Suboxone addiction is essential to take action in time and prevent serious consequences. This dependence can manifest through physical, emotional, and behavioral changes that often go unnoticed at first. Recognizing these signs allows for timely intervention and seeking professional help. Detecting the problem is the first step toward an effective and lasting recovery.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Suboxone addiction
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom1Item1: Wanting to cut down or stop using Suboxone but feeling unable to.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom1Item2: Ingesting Suboxone even though your pain has subsided.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom1Item3: Lying or exaggerating about pain to get prescribed Suboxone.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom1Item4: Taking Suboxone in response to stress, anxiety or boredom.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom1Item5: Needing higher doses of Suboxone to achieve the same effect.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom1Item7: Withdrawal symptoms when not taking the drug, such as: nausea and vomiting, diarrhea, sweating, chills, muscle aches, cramps, insomnia, restlessness, or agitation.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Suboxone addiction?
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom2Item1: Feeling physically ill or uncomfortable without the drug.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom2Item2: Changes in appearance such as fatigue and weight loss, and neglect of personal hygiene.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Suboxone addiction?
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom3Item1: Intense desire or obsession to take Suboxone.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom3Item2: Mood swings: alternating periods of euphoria, irritability, or depression.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Suboxone addiction?
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom4Item1: Using Suboxone despite the negative consequences to your finances, career, and relationships.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom4Item2: Engaging in risky behaviours such as driving under the influence or obtaining Suboxone illegally.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom4Item3: Visiting multiple doctors or chemists to obtain prescriptions.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom4Item4: Withdrawing from friends, family, and social activities.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom4Item5: Neglecting responsibilities at work, school, or home.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.symptom4Item6: Engaging in risky behaviors such as driving under the influence or obtaining Suboxone illegally.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot tell you exactly what your four-week stay will look like, as each person’s experience with Suboxone dependence is unique. At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that every recovery journey is different, which is why each client receives a fully personalized treatment plan tailored to their individual needs.
It is important to emphasize that we do not follow a rigid treatment model. Instead, we take a practical and flexible approach to addiction—focusing on what works best for you to achieve your goals.
Your time with us will be carefully designed to include a range of therapies specifically selected to support your recovery. We incorporate complementary treatments such as intravenous (IV) infusions, along with other specialized therapies, to make the process as comfortable as possible. Each therapy is designed to work together, addressing not only physical dependence but also the emotional and psychological aspects of addiction.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.section1Description:
We believe that every individual is unique, which is why we never use a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we carefully adapt each program to meet your specific needs. While we prioritize advanced techniques and a holistic approach, your personal circumstances guide everything we do.
At Gabriel Arcángel, we take a comprehensive and multidisciplinary approach. In addition to supporting you through the withdrawal process and managing your symptoms, we conduct in-depth assessments to understand the underlying causes of your Suboxone dependence. This allows us to design a highly personalized treatment plan focused on addressing root causes and achieving lasting recovery.
Your 360° comprehensive treatment plan includes a variety of therapies such as biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), bioresonance, Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, acupuncture, and equine-assisted therapy.
In addition to treating underlying factors, we also address the broader impact of dependence, such as potential neurological or physical damage. Our treatments typically integrate intensive psychotherapy with biochemical restoration and complementary therapies, all aimed at reducing stress and supporting long-term, sustainable recovery.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.section2Description:
We are a residential addiction treatment center in Querétaro that provides a dedicated team of professionals for each client.
During your stay, you will be supported by a team of experts including doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists, who provide 24/7 care. A key component is a live-in therapist available at all times to offer emotional support.
Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all are delivered with a high standard of medical excellence that sets us apart.
BuprenorphineSuboxone.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design our treatment programs according to each client’s specific needs. These may be physical and psychological; for example, if we identify nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions during the assessment, your program will be tailored accordingly.
We also consider practical needs, such as when a client needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
We provide treatment for Suboxone addiction, as well as co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, at our discreet treatment center in Querétaro.

HydrocodoneVicodinLortab.whatIsDescription:
Vicodin is an opioid that belongs to the narcotic analgesics group of medications. This powerful pain reliever works by reducing the perception of pain. It is commonly prescribed to treat pain severe enough to require opioid treatment or when other pain medications have not been effective. It may also be used to treat coughs. When used over extended periods, Vicodin can become addictive.
Physical dependence occurs when a person relies on Vicodin excessively. The body becomes so accustomed to the substance that, in its absence, it feels that something essential is missing. As a result, withdrawal symptoms appear. Psychological dependence arises when we begin to use Vicodin to escape or numb emotional pain, such as stress, loneliness, or trauma. Most individuals with prescription medication addiction experience varying levels of both physical and psychological dependence.
Over time, Vicodin abuse can cause serious harm to health. It increases the risk of respiratory depression, liver and kidney damage, seizures, and the development of other mental health disorders or substance use issues.
If you have developed a Vicodin addiction, it is not your fault, and recovery is possible. However, detoxing without professional and structured support can be very challenging. In many cases, the best option is to undergo a medical detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, along with support from mental health specialists to address the psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personalized care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that address both co-occurring conditions and all possible root causes of Vicodin addiction. In doing so, we can help you recover in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
