HydrocodoneVicodinLortab.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design each treatment program according to the specific needs of every client. These may be physical and psychological; for example, if nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions are identified during the assessment, the program will be tailored accordingly.
We also consider practical needs, such as when a client needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
We provide treatment for Vicodin addiction, as well as co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, at our private treatment center in Querétaro.

AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.conditionHeading: Adderall Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.conditionDescription: En Gabriel Arcángel, podemos ayudarte a superar tu adicción al Adderall y recuperar tu salud. Nuestros programas de tratamiento están diseñados para guiarte a través de una desintoxicación segura, controlar los síntomas de abstinencia y abordar cada causa de fondo de tu adicción.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.whatIsDescription:
El Adderall es una anfetamina. Las anfetaminas son potentes fármacos psicoestimulantes que afectan el sistema nervioso central. Se prescriben con frecuencia para tratar el TDAH (trastorno por déficit de atención e hiperactividad) y la narcolepsia. Sin embargo, también pueden usarse de forma recreativa e ilegal para aumentar la sensación de energía y estado de alerta. Además del Adderall, otras anfetaminas comunes incluyen Dexedrina, Vyvanse, metanfetamina y MDMA/éxtasis.
Cuando se utilizan de forma adecuada para tratar una condición de salud mental, las anfetaminas recetadas como el Adderall son seguras y pueden ser muy beneficiosas. Sin embargo, cuando una persona sin TDAH las consume, pueden generar un efecto eufórico que resulta adictivo. La vulnerabilidad a esta dependencia aumenta si la persona también enfrenta estrés, ansiedad u otros problemas de consumo de sustancias, los cuales suelen coexistir con el TDAH.
El Adderall es conocido por ser sobreprescrito, lo que significa que muchas personas lo consumen sin necesitarlo realmente, aumentando el riesgo de dependencia. Además, se puede desarrollar tolerancia rápidamente, lo que implica la necesidad de aumentar la dosis para obtener el mismo efecto. La dependencia a las anfetaminas puede tener consecuencias físicas y mentales graves.
Una vez consumido, el Adderall incrementa significativamente la actividad de neurotransmisores clave en el cerebro, como la norepinefrina y la dopamina. Estas sustancias mejoran la atención, el estado de alerta, el flujo sanguíneo, la motivación y el control motor. También generan sensaciones de euforia y recompensa que favorecen el consumo continuo. Sin embargo, estos efectos suelen ir seguidos de periodos de depresión, irritabilidad y agitación. Con el uso prolongado, este ciclo puede favorecer el desarrollo de diversos trastornos de salud mental.
Si has desarrollado una adicción al Adderall, es posible superarla. No obstante, desintoxicarse sin apoyo profesional y estructurado puede ser muy difícil. En muchos casos, la mejor opción es realizar una desintoxicación médica para controlar los síntomas físicos, junto con el apoyo de especialistas en salud mental para tratar los aspectos psicológicos.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.signsTitle: What are the signs of Adderall addiction?
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.signsDescription: Identifying the signs of Adderall addiction is essential to intervene early and prevent serious complications. This dependence can be reflected in physical, emotional, and behavioral changes that may seem harmless at first. Recognizing these signs allows for timely action and seeking professional support. Detecting the problem early is key to achieving an effective and lasting recovery.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Adderall addiction
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom1Item1: Querer reducir o dejar de usar Adderall pero sentirse incapaz de hacerlo.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom1Item2: Uso de Adderall en la vida cotidiana.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom1Item3: Tomar Adderall para lidiar con el estrés.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom1Item4: Pasar menos tiempo con la familia, amigos o haciendo actividades que antes disfrutabas.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom1Item5: Necesitar dosis cada vez mayores para obtener los mismos efectos placenteros.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom1Item7: Síntomas de abstinencia cuando no se toma el medicamento, como fatiga extrema o “colapsos”, trastornos del sueño (insomnio o hipersomnia), aumento del apetito, depresión, dolores de cabeza o dolores musculares.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Adderall addiction?
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom2Item1: Pérdida de apetito que lleva a cambios de peso notorios.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom2Item2: Problemas cardiovasculares como aumento de la frecuencia cardíaca, dolor torácico o hipertensión.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Adderall addiction?
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom3Item1: Deseo intenso u obsesión por tomar Adderall.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom3Item2: Cambios de humor: períodos alternados de euforia, irritabilidad, ansiedad o depresión.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom3Item3: Pensamientos acelerados o enfoque obsesivo en las tareas.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Adderall addiction?
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom4Item2: Uso de Adderall a pesar de las consecuencias negativas para tus finanzas, carrera y relaciones.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom4Item3: Visitas a varios médicos o farmacias para obtener recetas.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom4Item4: Estar excesivamente preocupado por obtener Adderall.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom4Item5: Acumular Adderall o priorizarlo sobre otras responsabilidades.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom4Item6: Retirarse de amigos, familia y actividades sociales.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom4Item7: Negligencia de responsabilidades en el trabajo, la escuela o el hogar.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.symptom4Item8: Participar en comportamientos arriesgados como conducir bajo la influencia u obtener Adderall ilegalmente.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.howWeTreatDescription:
No podemos decirte exactamente cómo será tu estancia de cuatro semanas, ya que cada experiencia con la adicción al Adderall es única. En Gabriel Arcángel, entendemos que cada proceso de recuperación es diferente, por lo que cada paciente recibe un plan de tratamiento completamente personalizado y adaptado a sus necesidades.
Asimismo, no seguimos un modelo de tratamiento rígido. En su lugar, adoptamos un enfoque práctico y flexible hacia la adicción: lo que mejor funcione para ayudarte a alcanzar tus objetivos.
Cada día de tu estancia estará cuidadosamente estructurado para incluir una variedad de terapias seleccionadas específicamente para ayudarte a superar tu adicción al Adderall. Esto incluye tratamientos como la terapia asistida con medicamentos (MAT) y sueros intravenosos (IV), con el objetivo de hacer el proceso de abstinencia lo más cómodo posible. Todas las terapias están diseñadas para complementarse entre sí, restaurando tu salud física, emocional y psicológica, y ayudándote a recuperar el control de tu vida.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.section1Description:
Entendemos que cada persona es única, por lo que rechazamos un enfoque generalizado. Cada programa de tratamiento se adapta cuidadosamente a tu situación y necesidades específicas. Aunque integramos técnicas terapéuticas avanzadas y un enfoque integral, tus necesidades son las que guían cada paso del proceso de recuperación.
Para quienes enfrentan dependencia al Adderall, el tratamiento comienza con un proceso de abstinencia supervisado cuidadosamente. Esto suele implicar una reducción gradual de la dosis para disminuir de forma segura los efectos de la abstinencia. En algunos casos, también utilizamos medicamentos específicos para apoyar la concentración y la función cognitiva durante la desintoxicación, así como para manejar síntomas como ansiedad, insomnio, irritabilidad o aumento de la frecuencia cardíaca.
Además de acompañarte durante la abstinencia, realizamos evaluaciones médicas y psicológicas profundas para identificar las causas de raíz de tu dependencia. Con base en estos hallazgos, diseñamos un plan personalizado para ayudarte a superarla de forma definitiva. También abordamos las consecuencias derivadas del consumo, desde daños neurológicos hasta afectaciones fisiológicas.
Tu programa personalizado se enfoca en psicoterapia intensiva, restauración bioquímica y terapias complementarias. Al atender tus necesidades físicas, psicológicas y bioquímicas, te guiamos hacia un proceso de recuperación integral y duradero.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.section2Description:
Somos un centro residencial de tratamiento de adicciones que ofrece un equipo dedicado de profesionales para cada paciente.
Gracias a una atención altamente personalizada, tratamos a cada paciente con un enfoque estructurado y cuidadosamente supervisado. El protocolo de desintoxicación es llevado a cabo por una enfermera clínica especializada y supervisado por nuestro psiquiatra. Además, un psicoterapeuta especializado en adicciones y una enfermera permanecen contigo las 24 horas.
El equipo se complementa con médicos de primer nivel, psiquiatras, especialistas en trauma y terapeutas complementarios. Independientemente de la combinación de tratamientos que recibas, todos se brindan con el alto nivel de excelencia médica que nos distingue.
AmphetamineDextroamphetamineAdderall.section3Description:
Cada uno de nuestros programas de tratamiento se diseña cuidadosamente de acuerdo con las necesidades específicas de cada paciente. Estas pueden ser físicas y psicológicas; por ejemplo, si durante la evaluación detectamos deficiencias nutricionales o signos de otras adicciones, el programa se ajusta en función de ello.
También consideramos necesidades prácticas, como cuando un paciente necesita trabajar o viajar durante su estancia con nosotros.
Ofrecemos tratamiento para la adicción al Adderall, así como para dependencias coexistentes y trastornos de salud mental, en nuestro centro privado de tratamiento en Querétaro.

MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.conditionHeading: Ritalin Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we are here to help you break free from Ritalin addiction, no matter how difficult it may seem. In a private and comfortable environment, we provide safe, expert-supervised support throughout the withdrawal process while working to address the root causes of your addiction and restore your health and well-being.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.whatIsDescription:
Methylphenidates such as Ritalin and Concerta are central nervous system (CNS) stimulants. They are commonly prescribed to treat ADHD (Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder) and narcolepsy. However, they can also be used recreationally and illegally to increase energy and alertness.
Ritalin works by increasing the activity of certain neurotransmitters in the brain, such as dopamine. As a result, a person may feel more focused, energized, attentive, and less impulsive or hyperactive. When someone has ADHD or narcolepsy, these effects help stabilize brain chemistry, making prescribed methylphenidates safe and beneficial. However, if these conditions are not present, or if the medication is taken in higher doses than prescribed, these effects can become highly addictive.
Additionally, these medications are often overprescribed, meaning many people take them without truly needing them, experiencing feelings of euphoria. Since the brain does not require this additional dopamine, it significantly increases the risk of developing an addiction.
For example, when someone who does not need stimulant medication takes methylphenidates like Ritalin, there is a significant increase in dopamine levels in the brain. In contrast, for those who do need it, this increase simply brings levels back to baseline without causing euphoria. When dopamine rises above baseline, the brain’s reward system reinforces that feeling, laying the foundation for addiction. Over time, tolerance and dependence on the stimulant can develop.
If you have developed a Ritalin addiction, it is possible to overcome it. However, detoxing without professional and structured support can be very challenging. In many cases, the best option is to undergo a medical detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, along with support from mental health specialists to address the psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personalized care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that address both co-occurring conditions and the root causes of Ritalin addiction. In doing so, we can help you recover in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.signsTitle: What are the signs of Ritalin addiction?
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.signsDescription: Identifying the signs of Ritalin addiction is key to intervening early and preventing serious consequences. This dependence can manifest through physical, emotional, and behavioral changes that may seem mild at first. Recognizing these signs allows for timely action and seeking professional help. Detecting the problem early is essential for achieving an effective and lasting recovery.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Ritalin addiction
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom1Item1: If prescribed, exceeding the recommended dosage.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom1Item2: Lying to doctors or exaggerating symptoms to obtain more Ritalin.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom1Item3: Continuing to use the medication despite wanting to quit.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom1Item4: Needing more and more Ritalin to get the desired effect.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom1Item5: Experiencing withdrawal cravings or physical symptoms when not taking the drug, such as fatigue or lethargy, depression, sleep disturbances (insomnia or hypersomnia), increased appetite, muscle aches, or headaches.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Ritalin addiction?
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom2Item1: Weight loss due to suppressed appetite.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom2Item2: Cardiovascular Issues such as increased heart rate, high blood pressure, or chest pain.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Ritalin addiction?
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom3Item1: Experiencing mood swings: alternating periods of euphoria, irritability, anxiety, or depression.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom3Item2: Obsessive focus or hyperactivity in mental processes, leading to burnout.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom3Item4: Increasing sense of anxiety or dread once the effects wear off
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom3Item5: Increasing use of other substances (alcohol, illicit drugs, nicotine, etc).
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Ritalin addiction?
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom4Item1: Using Ritalin despite the negative consequences to your finances, career, and relationships.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom4Item2: Visiting multiple doctors or chemists to obtain prescriptions.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom4Item3: Being overly preoccupied with obtaining Ritalin.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom4Item4: Stockpiling Ritalin or prioritising it over other responsibilities.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom4Item5: Withdrawing from friends, family, and social activities.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.symptom4Item6: Finding yourself unable to care about other responsibilities.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot tell you exactly what your four-week stay will look like, as each person’s experience with Ritalin addiction is unique and deeply personal. At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that every recovery journey is different, which is why each patient receives a fully personalized treatment plan tailored to their needs.
It is important to emphasize that we do not follow a rigid treatment model. Instead, we take a practical and flexible approach to addiction—focusing on what works best to help you achieve your goals.
Your stay with us will be carefully structured to include a variety of therapies specifically selected to help you overcome Ritalin addiction. From withdrawal to intensive therapy, every step is tailored to you. All therapies are designed to work together, restoring your physical, emotional, and psychological health and helping you regain control of your life.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.section1Description:
We understand that every journey is different, which is why we never use a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we carefully tailor each program to meet your specific needs. While we prioritize advanced methods and a holistic approach, your personal circumstances guide the entire process.
As part of your recovery from Ritalin, we provide a carefully supervised withdrawal process supported by medication-assisted treatment (MAT) and intravenous (IV) infusions to ease withdrawal symptoms. Another key component of treatment is in-depth assessments to identify the root causes of your dependence. This allows us to design a personalized plan focused on long-term recovery.
Your program will include therapies such as biofeedback, bioresonance, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, acupuncture, and art therapy.
In addition to treating underlying factors, we also address the broader impact of dependence, including potential neurological or physical damage caused by substance use. Our treatments integrate intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, aiming to achieve a strong and lasting recovery.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.section2Description:
We are a residential addiction treatment center in Querétaro that provides a dedicated team of professionals for each patient.
Your recovery process will be guided by a team of experts. A specialized clinical nurse and a psychiatrist will carry out and supervise your withdrawal process. In addition, an addiction psychotherapist and a nurse will stay with you 24/7.
The team is further supported by top-level doctors, psychiatrists, trauma specialists, and complementary therapists who provide continuous care. Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all are delivered with the high standard of medical excellence that defines us.
MethylphenidateRitalinConcerta.section3Description:
Each of our treatment programs is carefully designed according to the specific needs of every patient. These may be physical and psychological; for example, if nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions are identified during the assessment, the program will be tailored accordingly.
We also consider practical needs, such as when a patient needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
We provide treatment for Ritalin addiction, as well as co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, at our discreet treatment center in Querétaro.

AlprazolamXanax.conditionHeading: Xanax Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
AlprazolamXanax.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we are here to help you break free from Xanax addiction and regain your health, no matter how challenging it may seem. Our personalized treatment programs provide safe support during detox, help manage symptoms, and address the underlying causes of addiction.
AlprazolamXanax.whatIsDescription:
Xanax is classified as a benzodiazepine. This group of medications are sedatives designed to treat anxiety, insomnia, epilepsy, stress, and panic attacks. They work by enhancing the effects of the neurotransmitter GABA in the brain. GABA is a calming neurotransmitter, and billions of brain cells respond to its signals. When a person feels excessively anxious, the brain becomes overactive. Xanax contains compounds that counteract this overactivity, helping maintain a more relaxed state. However, as a result, benzodiazepines like Xanax are highly addictive and can lead to strong physical and psychological dependence, as well as various emotional issues.
Xanax is one of the most commonly misused benzodiazepines. Because it reduces activity in the central nervous system (CNS), individuals who take higher-than-recommended doses may become more passive and apathetic. The more the medication is abused, the stronger the feeling of being unable to function without it. This can quickly lead to tolerance, resulting in higher doses and dependency.
When sedatives like Xanax are combined with other central nervous system depressants, such as alcohol, the CNS can become severely depressed. This system regulates vital functions such as breathing and heart rate. If it becomes too depressed, it may stop functioning properly, leading to difficulty breathing or a dangerously slowed heart rate.
If you have developed a Xanax addiction, it is possible to overcome it. However, detoxing without professional and structured support can be extremely difficult. In many cases, the best option is to undergo a medical detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, along with support from mental health specialists to address the psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personalized care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that address both co-occurring conditions and all possible root causes of Xanax addiction. In doing so, we can help you recover in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
AlprazolamXanax.signsTitle: What are the signs of Xanax addiction?
AlprazolamXanax.signsDescription: Identifying the signs of Xanax addiction is essential to intervene early and prevent serious health risks. This dependence can manifest through physical, emotional, and behavioral changes that often go unnoticed at first. Recognizing these signs allows for timely action and seeking professional help. Detecting the problem early is key to achieving a safe and lasting recovery.
AlprazolamXanax.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Xanax addiction
AlprazolamXanax.symptom1Item1: Continuing to use Xanax despite a wish to stop.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom1Item2: Needing to increase the dosage to achieve the desired effect.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom1Item3: Isolating yourself from friends or loved ones.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom1Item4: Partaking in dangerous and risky behaviour to obtain the drug.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom1Item5: The development of withdrawal symptoms, such as anxiety, panic attacks, insomnia, tremors, muscle aches, nausea, vomiting, or seizures (in severe cases).
AlprazolamXanax.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Xanax addiction?
AlprazolamXanax.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Xanax addiction?
AlprazolamXanax.symptom3Item1: A strong desire to take Xanax, even without medical need.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom3Item2: Emotional numbness: a sense of detachment or lack of emotional response.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom3Item3: Mood swings: periods of euphoria, irritability, or depression.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom3Item4: Cognitive impairment such as memory problems, confusion, or difficulty concentrating.
AlprazolamXanax.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Xanax addiction?
AlprazolamXanax.symptom4Item1: Concealing Xanax pills or lying about usage.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom4Item2: Using Xanax despite the negative consequences to your finances, career and relationships.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom4Item3: Taking more Xanax than prescribed or using it recreationally.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom4Item4: Visiting multiple doctors or chemists to obtain additional prescriptions.
AlprazolamXanax.symptom4Item5: Neglecting work, school, or family responsibilities due to drug use.
AlprazolamXanax.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot define exactly what your four-week stay will look like, as there is no single treatment path. At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that each person’s experience with Xanax addiction is deeply personal. Therefore, every patient receives a fully personalized treatment plan designed according to their needs.
It is important to emphasize that we do not follow a rigid treatment model. Instead, we take a practical and flexible approach to addiction—focusing on what works best to help you achieve your goals.
Each day of your stay will be carefully structured to include a variety of therapies tailored specifically to you. These therapies work together to restore your physical, emotional, and psychological well-being, helping you regain balance and control of your life. From withdrawal management to intensive therapy, every aspect is designed around you.
AlprazolamXanax.section1Description:
We understand that every journey is different, which is why we never use a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we carefully tailor each program to meet your specific needs. While we prioritize advanced methods and a holistic approach, your personal circumstances guide the entire process.
As part of your recovery from Xanax, we provide a carefully supervised withdrawal process supported by medication-assisted treatment (MAT) and intravenous (IV) infusions to ease symptoms. Another key component is conducting in-depth assessments to identify the underlying causes of your dependence. This allows us to design a personalized plan focused on long-term recovery.
Your program will include therapies such as biofeedback, bioresonance, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, acupuncture, and equine-assisted therapy.
In addition to addressing root causes, we also focus on the broader impact of addiction, including potential neurological or physical damage. Our treatments integrate intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, aiming to reduce stress and support a strong, long-term recovery.
AlprazolamXanax.section2Description:
We are a residential addiction treatment center in Querétaro that provides a dedicated team of professionals for each patient.
Your recovery process will be guided by a team of doctors, psychiatrists, psychotherapists, addiction specialists, and complementary therapists. The withdrawal protocol is carefully planned by our medical team and supervised by a 24/7 nursing staff. In addition, a live-in therapist will be available at all times to provide emotional support.
Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all are delivered with the high standard of medical excellence that defines us.
AlprazolamXanax.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design comprehensive and intensive treatment programs tailored to each individual. Based on principles of empathy and practicality, we create a plan aligned with your specific needs. These may be physical and psychological; for example, if nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions are identified during the assessment, the program is adjusted accordingly.
We also consider practical needs, such as when a patient needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
We provide treatment for Xanax addiction, as well as co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, at our discreet treatment center in Querétaro.

DiazepamValium.conditionHeading: Xanax Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
DiazepamValium.conditionDescription: Valium is one of the most addictive and potentially dangerous prescription medications today. At Gabriel Arcángel, we can help you overcome your Valium addiction, no matter how difficult it may seem. Our comprehensive and practical treatment programs are designed to support you through a safe detox and address each underlying cause of the addiction.
DiazepamValium.whatIsDescription:
Valium is classified as a benzodiazepine. These medications are sedatives designed to treat anxiety, alcohol withdrawal, muscle spasms, and seizures. Valium works by enhancing the effects of the neurotransmitter GABA in the brain. GABA is a calming neurotransmitter, and billions of brain cells respond to its signals. When a person feels excessively anxious, the brain becomes overactive. Valium contains compounds that counteract this overactivity, helping maintain a more relaxed state. However, for those dealing with constant stress—whether emotional, psychological, or social—benzodiazepines like Valium can become highly addictive, as they temporarily mask that distress. As a result, misuse often leads to strong physical and psychological dependence, as well as various emotional issues.
Valium is often used by individuals trying to cope with daily stress. However, because it reduces activity in the central nervous system (CNS), those who take higher-than-recommended doses may become more passive and apathetic. The more it is misused, the stronger the feeling of being unable to function without it. This can quickly lead to tolerance, resulting in higher doses and dependency.
When sedatives like Valium are combined with other central nervous system depressants, such as alcohol, the CNS can become severely depressed. This system regulates vital functions like breathing and heart rate. If it becomes too suppressed, it may stop functioning properly, leading to difficulty breathing or a dangerously slowed heart rate.
If you have developed a Valium addiction, it is not only possible to overcome it, but also to learn how to manage the underlying factors that led to your dependence. In other words, it’s not just about stopping the medication—it’s about transforming your life to achieve a state of well-being, calm, and sobriety.
However, detoxing from Valium without professional and structured support can be extremely difficult. In many cases, the safest approach is a medical detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, along with support from mental health professionals to address the psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personalized care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that address both co-occurring conditions and all underlying causes of addiction.
DiazepamValium.signsTitle: What are the signs of Xanax addiction?
DiazepamValium.signsDescription: Identifying the signs of Valium addiction is essential to intervene early and prevent serious consequences. This dependence can manifest through physical, emotional, and behavioral changes that may go unnoticed at first. Recognizing these signs allows for timely action and seeking professional help. Detecting the problem early is key to achieving a safe and lasting recovery.
DiazepamValium.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Xanax addiction
DiazepamValium.symptom1Item1: Continuar usando Valium a pesar del deseo de parar.
DiazepamValium.symptom1Item2: Necesidad de aumentar la dosis para lograr el efecto deseado.
DiazepamValium.symptom1Item3: Sentir como si estuvieras perdiendo energía para una vida “sobria” (p.ej., aislarte de amigos o seres queridos).
DiazepamValium.symptom1Item4: Participar en comportamientos peligrosos y arriesgados para obtener el medicamento.
DiazepamValium.symptom1Item5: Desarrollo de síntomas de abstinencia como ansiedad, ataques de pánico, dolor o rigidez muscular, temblores, sudoración, escalofríos, insomnio, náuseas o vómitos y convulsiones (en casos graves).
DiazepamValium.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Xanax addiction?
DiazepamValium.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Xanax addiction?
DiazepamValium.symptom3Item1: Paranoia o ansiedad y empeoramiento de la ansiedad cuando no se usa Valium, incluso si fue recetado inicialmente para la ansiedad.
DiazepamValium.symptom3Item2: Deterioro de la memoria como dificultad para retener información o recordar eventos.
DiazepamValium.symptom3Item3: Cambios de humor: fluctuaciones entre euforia, irritabilidad o depresión.
DiazepamValium.symptom3Item4: Confusión cognitiva como problemas para concentrarse o tomar decisiones.
DiazepamValium.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Xanax addiction?
DiazepamValium.symptom4Item1: Uso de Valium a pesar de las consecuencias negativas para tus finanzas, carrera y relaciones.
DiazepamValium.symptom4Item2: Tomar más Valium del recetado o usarlo recreativamente.
DiazepamValium.symptom4Item3: Visitar a varios médicos o farmacéuticos para obtener recetas adicionales.
DiazepamValium.symptom4Item4: Descuidar el trabajo, la escuela o las responsabilidades familiares debido al uso de drogas.
DiazepamValium.symptom4Item5: Comportamientos arriesgados: mezclar Valium con alcohol u otras drogas, aumentando el riesgo de sobredosis.
DiazepamValium.symptom4Item6: Ocultar pastillas de Valium o mentir sobre su uso.
DiazepamValium.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot say exactly what your four-week stay will look like, as there is no single treatment plan. At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that every recovery journey is different, which is why each patient receives a fully personalized treatment plan. From withdrawal management to intensive therapy, every stage is tailored to your individual needs.
It is important to emphasize that we do not follow a rigid treatment model. Instead, we take a practical and flexible approach to addiction—focusing on what works best to help you achieve your goals.
Each day is carefully structured to include a range of therapeutic interventions designed to support your recovery and help you develop healthier coping mechanisms for stress and discomfort. These therapies work together to restore your physical, emotional, and psychological well-being.
DiazepamValium.section1Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we believe that every journey is unique, which is why we avoid a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we tailor each program to your specific needs. Our approach combines advanced therapies with holistic care, always guided by your personal circumstances.
For Valium dependency, treatment begins with a carefully supervised withdrawal plan. Stopping abruptly can be dangerous, so a gradual tapering process under medical supervision helps minimize withdrawal symptoms. In some cases, specific medications are also used to ease the transition and manage symptoms such as insomnia and anxiety.
In addition to supporting you through this process, we conduct in-depth medical and psychological assessments to identify the root causes of your dependency. Based on these findings, we design a personalized plan focused on long-term recovery. We also address the consequences of substance use, such as neurological or physiological damage.
Your personalized program will integrate intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies—addressing your physical, psychological, and biochemical needs to guide you toward lasting recovery.
DiazepamValium.section2Description:
We are a residential addiction treatment center that provides a dedicated team of professionals for each patient.
Your recovery will be guided by a strong team of doctors, psychiatrists, psychotherapists, and complementary therapists. The withdrawal protocol is carefully designed by our medical team and supervised by a 24/7 nursing staff. Additionally, a live-in therapist will be available at all times to provide emotional support.
Regardless of the combination of treatments you receive, all are delivered with the high standard of medical excellence that defines us.
DiazepamValium.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design comprehensive and intensive treatment programs tailored to each individual. Guided by principles of empathy and practicality, we create a plan based on your specific needs. These may be physical and psychological; for example, if nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions are identified during the assessment, your program will be adjusted accordingly.
We also consider practical needs, such as when a patient needs to work or travel during their stay with us.
We provide treatment for Valium addiction, as well as co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, at our discreet treatment center in Querétaro.

LorazepamAtivan.conditionHeading: Ativan Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
LorazepamAtivan.conditionDescription: Ativan is a highly addictive benzodiazepine commonly prescribed to treat anxiety or insomnia. Because it is a prescribed medication, it can create a false sense of security—how could something recommended by a doctor be addictive? If you’ve come to recognize that you depend on Ativan, it’s important to know that you are not alone and that help is available. At Gabriel Arcángel, we are here to support you in overcoming your Ativan addiction, no matter how overwhelming it may feel.
LorazepamAtivan.whatIsDescription:
Benzodiazepines like Ativan are sedatives designed to treat conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and epilepsy. Ativan works by enhancing the effects of the neurotransmitter GABA in the brain. GABA is a calming neurotransmitter, and billions of brain cells respond to its signals. When a person feels overly anxious, the brain becomes overactive. Ativan contains compounds that counteract this overactivity, helping to maintain a more relaxed state. However, due to this effect, benzodiazepines like Ativan can become highly addictive, especially for individuals living in a constant state of stress or alertness. This can lead to strong physical and psychological dependence, as well as various emotional issues.
Because Ativan reduces activity in the central nervous system (CNS), individuals who take higher-than-recommended doses may become more passive, slow, or apathetic. The more the medication is misused, the stronger the feeling of not being able to function without it. This can quickly lead to tolerance, requiring higher doses and eventually resulting in dependency.
Ativan decreases brain activity by depressing the central nervous system. When taken in excess, it can cause extreme drowsiness, lack of motivation, and difficulty performing daily activities. Over time, misuse makes it increasingly difficult to function without the substance, reinforcing the cycle of dependence.
Combining Ativan with other central nervous system depressants, such as alcohol, poses serious risks. The CNS regulates vital functions like breathing and heart rate. If it becomes overly depressed, the body may struggle to maintain these functions, potentially leading to slow breathing, dangerous heart rate irregularities, or even complete failure.
If you are facing an Ativan addiction, recovery is possible. However, detoxing can be very difficult without professional support. A medical detox helps manage the physical symptoms of withdrawal, while mental health professionals address the psychological aspects, guiding you toward lasting recovery.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personalized care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that address both co-occurring conditions and all underlying root causes of Ativan addiction.
LorazepamAtivan.signsTitle: What are the signs of Ativan addiction?
LorazepamAtivan.signsDescription: Recognizing the signs of Ativan addiction is essential to intervene early and prevent serious consequences. This dependency can manifest through physical, emotional, and behavioral changes that may initially be subtle. Identifying these signs allows for timely action and seeking professional help. Detecting the problem early is key to achieving an effective and lasting recovery.
LorazepamAtivan.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Ativan addiction
LorazepamAtivan.symptom1Item1: Isolating yourself from friends or loved ones.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom1Item2: Partaking in dangerous and risky behaviour to obtain the drug.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom1Item3: Continuing to use Ativan despite a wish to stop.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom1Item4: Needing to increase the dosage to achieve the desired effect.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom1Item5: The development of withdrawal symptoms, such as anxiety, panic attacks, irritability, insomnia, restless sleep, sweating, chills, tremors, nausea, vomiting, appetite changes and seizures (in severe cases).
LorazepamAtivan.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Ativan addiction?
LorazepamAtivan.symptom2Item1: Coordination Issues: dizziness or difficulty maintaining balance.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom2Item2: Loss of control of body movements.
LorazepamAtivan.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Ativan addiction?
LorazepamAtivan.symptom3Item1: An intense urge or compulsion to use Ativan, even when inappropriate.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom3Item2: Mood swings: unpredictable changes in mood, including irritability and depression.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom3Item3: Memory impairment such as short-term memory loss or difficulty recalling events.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom3Item4: Cognitive impairment such as trouble concentrating or maintaining focus.
LorazepamAtivan.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Ativan addiction?
LorazepamAtivan.symptom4Item1: Using Ativan despite the negative consequences to your finances, career and relationships.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom4Item2: Taking more Ativan than prescribed or using it recreationally.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom4Item3: Visiting multiple doctors or chemists to obtain additional prescriptions.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom4Item4: Neglecting work, school, or family responsibilities due to drug use.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom4Item5: Risky behaviors: mixing Ativan with alcohol or other drugs, increasing the risk of overdose.
LorazepamAtivan.symptom4Item6: Concealing Ativan pills or lying about usage.

While we cannot define exactly what your four-week stay will look like, we can assure you that every part of your treatment is designed to help you break the cycle of addiction. It is important to emphasize that we do not follow a rigid treatment model. Instead, we take a practical and flexible approach—whatever works best to help you achieve your goals.
Your stay will be carefully structured to include a variety of therapeutic interventions specifically selected to support you. From withdrawal management to intensive therapy, every step is tailored to you. All therapies work together to restore your physical, emotional, and psychological well-being.

Treatment begins with a carefully supervised withdrawal process. This typically involves a gradual dose reduction under medical supervision to minimize symptoms. In some cases, specific medications are also used to manage symptoms such as insomnia or anxiety.
In addition, we conduct in-depth medical and psychological assessments to identify the root causes of your dependency. Based on these findings, we create a personalized plan to help you overcome it for good. We also address the consequences of substance use, such as potential neurological or physiological damage.
Your program will include intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, addressing your physical, psychological, and biochemical needs to guide you toward lasting recovery.

ClonazepamKlonopin.conditionHeading: Klonopin Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
ClonazepamKlonopin.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we are here to help you break free from Klonopin addiction, no matter how difficult it may seem. Our comprehensive and practical treatment programmes provide safe support during detox, address the underlying causes of addiction, and help you restore your health and well-being.
ClonazepamKlonopin.whatIsDescription:
Klonopin is a benzodiazepine that can be highly addictive. Benzodiazepines like Klonopin are sedatives designed to treat conditions such as anxiety, panic attacks, and seizures. Klonopin works by enhancing the effects of the neurotransmitter GABA in the brain. GABA is a calming neurotransmitter, and billions of brain cells respond to its signals. When a person feels overly anxious, the brain becomes hyperactive. Klonopin contains compounds that counteract this overactivity, helping maintain a more relaxed state. However, due to this effect, benzodiazepines like Klonopin can be highly addictive and may lead to strong physical and psychological dependence, as well as various emotional issues.
Because Klonopin reduces activity in the central nervous system (CNS), individuals who take higher-than-recommended doses may become more passive, slow, or apathetic. The more the medication is misused or the prescribed dose is exceeded, the stronger the feeling of being unable to function without it becomes. This can quickly lead to tolerance, resulting in higher doses and eventual dependence. Due to the potency of the drug, addiction can also develop even when taken as prescribed.
When sedatives like Klonopin are combined with other central nervous system depressants, such as alcohol, the CNS can become severely suppressed. This system regulates vital functions like breathing and heart rate. If overly depressed, it may fail to function properly, leading to difficulty breathing, respiratory arrest, or a dangerously slowed heart rate.
If you have developed a Klonopin addiction, recovery is possible. However, detoxing without professional and structured support can be extremely difficult. In many cases, the best option is a medical detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, along with support from mental health specialists to address psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personalised care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that address both co-occurring conditions and all underlying root causes of Klonopin addiction. This allows us to help you recover in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
ClonazepamKlonopin.signsTitle: What are the signs of Klonopin addiction?
ClonazepamKlonopin.signsDescription: Recognising the signs of Klonopin addiction is essential for early intervention and preventing serious complications. This dependency may present through physical, emotional, and behavioural changes that can initially seem subtle. Identifying these signs allows for timely action and seeking professional help. Detecting the problem early is key to achieving an effective and lasting recovery.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Klonopin addiction
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom1Item1: Continuing to use Klonopin despite a wish to stop.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom1Item2: Needing to increase the dosage to achieve the desired effect.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom1Item3: Partaking in dangerous and risky behaviour to obtain the drug.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom1Item4: The development of withdrawal symptoms, such as anxiety, agitation, insomnia, tremors, nausea, or seizures when not taking the drug.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom1Item5: Isolating yourself from friends or loved ones.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Klonopin addiction?
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom2Item1: Coordination issues such as trouble walking or performing physical tasks.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Klonopin addiction?
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom3Item1: An intense urge or compulsion to use Klonopin, even when inappropriate.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom3Item2: Anxiety or paranoia: heightened anxiety despite using the drug, or developing irrational fears.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom3Item3: Cognitive impairment such as trouble concentrating, memory problems and confusion.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom3Item4: Mood swings: frequent irritability, agitation, or depressive episodes.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Klonopin addiction?
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom4Item1: Using Klonopin despite the negative consequences to your finances, career, and relationships.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom4Item2: Taking more Klonopin than prescribed or using it recreationally.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom4Item3: Visiting multiple doctors or chemists to obtain additional prescriptions.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom4Item4: Neglecting work, school, or family responsibilities due to drug use.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom4Item5: Risky behaviours: mixing Klonopin with alcohol or other drugs, increasing the risk of overdose.
ClonazepamKlonopin.symptom4Item6: Concealing Klonopin pills or lying about usage.
ClonazepamKlonopin.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot say exactly what your four-week stay will look like, as every recovery journey is unique. At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that each person requires a fully personalised treatment plan tailored to their needs. We do not follow a rigid model or predefined path; instead, we take a practical and flexible approach—whatever works best to help you achieve your goals.
Each day is carefully structured to include a range of therapies specifically selected to support your recovery. From withdrawal management to intensive therapy, every stage is tailored to you. All therapies work together to address not only physical dependence but also the emotional and psychological aspects of addiction.
ClonazepamKlonopin.section1Description:
We recognise that every journey is different, which is why we avoid a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we adapt each programme to your specific needs. While we prioritise advanced methods and a holistic approach, your personal circumstances guide the entire process.
As part of your recovery, we provide a carefully supervised withdrawal process, incorporating medication-assisted treatment (MAT) and IV therapy to make detox more comfortable. A key component is conducting in-depth assessments to identify the factors behind your dependency. Based on this, we design a fully personalised plan focused on long-term recovery.
Your programme will include therapies such as biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behaviour Therapy (DBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, EMDR, acupuncture, and equine-assisted therapy.
In addition to addressing root causes, we also focus on the broader impact of Klonopin use, including possible neurological or physical imbalances. Our comprehensive approach combines intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies to reduce stress, enhance well-being, and support a strong, lasting recovery.
ClonazepamKlonopin.section2Description:
We are a residential addiction treatment centre that provides a dedicated team of professionals for each patient.
Your recovery will be guided by a team of doctors, psychiatrists, psychotherapists, and complementary therapists. The withdrawal protocol is carefully designed by our medical team and supervised by nursing staff available 24/7. In addition, you will have a live-in therapist providing continuous emotional support.
All treatments are delivered with the high standard of medical excellence that defines us.
ClonazepamKlonopin.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design comprehensive and intensive treatment programmes tailored to each individual, based on principles of empathy and practicality.
We provide treatment for Klonopin addiction, as well as co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, at our discreet treatment centre in Querétaro.

ZolpidemAmbien.conditionHeading: Ambien Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
ZolpidemAmbien.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we can help you overcome your Ambien addiction, no matter how difficult it may seem. Our comprehensive and practical treatment programmes are designed to support you through a safe Ambien detox, address each underlying cause of addiction, and help you restore your health.
ZolpidemAmbien.whatIsDescription: Ambien is a powerful sedative commonly prescribed to treat insomnia and other sleep disorders. Although it is often considered less addictive than benzodiazepines such as Xanax, taking it in high doses or over extended periods can lead to addiction. Commonly known as a “sleeping pill,” Ambien is classified as a hypnotic drug within the group of sedative-hypnotics. These medications are also referred to as “z-drugs” due to their ability to induce sleep.
ZolpidemAmbien.signsTitle: What are the signs of Ambien addiction?
ZolpidemAmbien.signsDescription: It can be difficult to recognise an Ambien addiction, as we do not usually associate substance abuse with prescribed or over-the-counter medications.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Ambien addiction
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom1Item1: Continuing to use Ambien despite a wish to stop.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom1Item2: Needing to increase the dosage to achieve the desired effect.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom1Item3: Isolating yourself from friends or loved ones.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom1Item4: Partaking in dangerous and risky behaviour to obtain the drug.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom1Item5: The development of withdrawal symptoms, such as anxiety, agitation, nausea, tremors, sweating, and, in severe cases, seizures.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Ambien addiction?
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom2Item3: Impaired coordination such as trouble with balance and fine motor skills, increasing the risk of falls or accidents.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Ambien addiction?
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom3Item1: A strong desire to take Ambien, even without medical need.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom3Item2: Feeling unable to fall asleep or function without Ambien.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom3Item3: Mood swings: unpredictable changes in mood, irritability, or emotional instability.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom3Item4: Memory problems such as short-term memory loss, including forgetting conversations or events (‘anterograde amnesia’).
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom3Item5: Rebound insomnia: increased difficulty sleeping when you stop taking Ambien.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Ambien addiction?
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom4Item1: Using Ambien despite the negative consequences to your finances, career and relationships.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom4Item2: Taking Ambien during the day as a coping mechanism or to experience its sedative effects.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom4Item3: Taking more Ambien than prescribed or using it recreationally.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom4Item4: Visiting multiple doctors or chemists to obtain additional prescriptions.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom4Item5: Neglecting work, school, or family responsibilities due to drug use.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom4Item6: Risky behaviours: mixing Ambien with alcohol or other drugs, increasing the risk of overdose.
ZolpidemAmbien.symptom4Item7: Concealing Ambien pills or lying about usage.
ZolpidemAmbien.howWeTreatDescription:
We cannot say exactly what your four-week stay will look like, as each individual’s experience with Ambien addiction is unique. For this reason, we design a fully personalised treatment plan for every individual; there is no predefined path. It is also important to emphasise that we do not follow a rigid model. Instead, we take a practical and flexible approach to addiction—whatever works best to help you achieve your goals.
Each day is carefully structured to include a variety of therapeutic interventions specifically selected to help you overcome your Ambien addiction and develop healthier coping mechanisms. From withdrawal management to intensive therapy, every step is tailored to you. All therapies work together to restore your physical, emotional, and psychological health, helping you regain control of your life.
ZolpidemAmbien.section1Description:
We understand that every journey is different, which is why we never take a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we carefully tailor each programme to your specific needs. While we prioritise advanced methods and a holistic approach, your personal circumstances guide the entire process.
As part of your recovery from Ambien, we provide a carefully supervised withdrawal process supported by medication-assisted treatment (MAT) and IV therapy to ease symptoms. Another key component of treatment is conducting in-depth assessments to identify the underlying causes of your dependency. Based on these insights, we design a personalised plan focused on long-term recovery.
Your programme will include therapies such as biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behaviour Therapy (DBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, physical training, acupuncture, and art therapy.
In addition to addressing the root causes, we also evaluate the broader impact of Ambien use, treating any neurological or physical damage that may have developed. Our treatments typically combine intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies, with the goal of reducing stress and supporting a strong, sustainable recovery.
ZolpidemAmbien.section2Description:
We are a residential addiction treatment centre that provides a dedicated team of professionals for each patient.
Our team is made up of doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists. Through highly personalised care, we offer continuous support to guide you through withdrawal and into recovery. You will also have access to a live-in therapist available 24/7 for emotional support.
All treatments are delivered with the high standard of medical excellence that defines us.
ZolpidemAmbien.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design comprehensive and intensive treatment programmes tailored to each individual, based on principles of empathy and practicality. These needs may be physical and psychological; for example, if during the assessment we identify nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions, the programme is adjusted accordingly. We also consider practical needs, such as when a patient needs to work or travel during their stay.
We provide treatment for Ambien addiction, as well as co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, at our discreet treatment centre in Querétaro.

GabapentinNeurontin.conditionHeading: Neurontin Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
GabapentinNeurontin.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand how difficult it can be to face a Neurontin addiction. But you don’t have to go through it alone; we’re here to help. Our comprehensive and practical treatment programmes provide safe, expert support during detox, address the underlying causes of addiction, and help you restore your health.
GabapentinNeurontin.whatIsDescription:
Neurontin is an anticonvulsant that belongs to a class of medications known as gabapentinoids. It is commonly prescribed to treat nerve pain, restless leg syndrome, alcohol and cocaine withdrawal, fibromyalgia, and seizures. Due to its ability to treat a wide range of conditions, it is one of the most widely used medications. Although it is often considered a less addictive alternative to opioids, it can still lead to dependency.
Neurontin works by modifying calcium channels to reduce seizures and relieve nerve pain. It has a chemical structure similar to the neurotransmitter GABA in the brain. GABA is a calming neurotransmitter, and billions of brain cells respond to its signals. When a person feels overly anxious, the brain becomes overactive. Neurontin helps counteract this overactivity, promoting a calmer state. As a result, it can produce feelings of relaxation, tranquility, and even euphoria when taken in higher doses.
Because of these effects, Neurontin can be addictive and may lead to strong physical and psychological dependence, as well as various emotional issues. With prolonged use, the brain adapts, requiring higher doses to achieve the same effects—this is known as tolerance.
It is also common for some individuals to combine Neurontin with opioids to enhance its effects, which poses a very high risk and can lead to a fatal overdose. Unlike opioids, there is currently no specific antidote to reverse a gabapentin overdose.
If you have developed a Neurontin addiction, recovery is possible. However, detoxing without professional, structured support can be challenging. In many cases, the safest approach is a medically supervised detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, along with support from mental health professionals to address the psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personalised care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that address both co-occurring conditions and all underlying root causes of Neurontin addiction. This allows us to help you recover in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
GabapentinNeurontin.signsTitle: What are the signs of Neurontin addiction?
GabapentinNeurontin.signsDescription: It can be difficult to identify a Neurontin addiction, as substance misuse is not commonly associated with prescribed or over-the-counter medications.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Neurontin addiction
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom1Item1: Continuing to use Neurontin despite a wish to stop.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom1Item2: Needing to increase the dosage to achieve the desired effect.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom1Item3: Partaking in dangerous and risky behaviour to obtain the drug.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom1Item4: The development of withdrawal symptoms, such as anxiety, restlessness, agitation, insomnia, sweating, chills, nausea, vomiting, increased sensitivity to pain, or seizures when not taking the drug.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom1Item5: Isolating yourself from friends or loved ones.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Neurontin addiction?
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom2Item4: Coordination problems such as clumsiness or difficulty with fine motor skills.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom2Item5: Visual disturbances or inability to focus.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom2Item6: Swelling or puffiness in hands, feet, or ankles (peripheral edema).
GabapentinNeurontin.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Neurontin addiction?
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom3Item4: Coordination problems such as clumsiness or difficulty with fine motor skills.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom3Item5: Visual disturbances or inability to focus.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom3Item6: Swelling or puffiness in hands, feet, or ankles (peripheral edema).
GabapentinNeurontin.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Neurontin addiction?
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom4Item1: Using Neurontin despite the negative consequences to your finances, career and relationships.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom4Item2: Taking more Neurontin than prescribed or using it recreationally.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom4Item3: Visiting multiple doctors or chemists to obtain additional prescriptions.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom4Item4: Neglecting work, school, or family responsibilities due to drug use.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom4Item5: Risky behaviours: mixing Neurontin with alcohol or other drugs, increasing the risk of overdose.
GabapentinNeurontin.symptom4Item6: Concealing Neurontin pills or lying about usage.

Your stay will be carefully structured to include a range of therapies specifically selected to address your Neurontin dependency. Every stage, from withdrawal management to intensive therapy, is tailored to you. All therapies work together to heal not only the physical aspects of addiction but also the emotional and psychological factors involved.
GabapentinNeurontin.section1Description:
We understand that every journey is different, which is why we never take a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we carefully tailor each programme to your specific needs. While we prioritise advanced treatment methods and holistic care, your individual circumstances guide everything we do.
As part of your recovery, we provide a carefully supervised withdrawal process, incorporating medication-assisted treatment (MAT) and IV therapy to make the experience as comfortable as possible. Based on these insights, we design a fully personalised treatment plan focused on long-term recovery.
Your programme may include therapies such as biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behaviour Therapy (DBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, EMDR, acupuncture, and art therapy.
In addition to addressing root causes, we also assess and treat any neurological or physical damage resulting from Neurontin use. Our integrated approach combines intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies to reduce stress and support a strong, sustainable recovery.
GabapentinNeurontin.section2Description:

CarisoprodolSoma.conditionHeading: Soma Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
CarisoprodolSoma.conditionDescription: At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand how overwhelming it can be to face a Soma addiction. You don’t have to go through this alone; we’re here to help. Our comprehensive and practical treatment programmes support you through a safe detox from Soma, address the root causes of the addiction, and help you restore your health and well-being.
CarisoprodolSoma.whatIsDescription:
Carisoprodol (Soma) is a centrally acting muscle relaxant. It is commonly prescribed to treat pain caused by muscle injuries such as strains and sprains, as well as general pain, and in some cases to help with sleep. Although it was widely used in the past, in recent years it has largely been replaced by benzodiazepines. When taken over extended periods or in high doses, it can lead to addiction.
Soma works directly on the central nervous system (CNS), blocking pain signals between the brain and the nerves. It has a chemical structure similar to the neurotransmitter GABA in the brain. GABA is a calming neurotransmitter, and billions of brain cells respond to its signals. When a person feels overly anxious, the brain becomes overactive. Soma helps counteract this overactivity, keeping the brain in a more relaxed state. As a result, it produces muscle relaxation, sedation, reduced anxiety, and at higher doses, a mild sense of euphoria.
Due to these effects, Soma can be highly addictive and may lead to strong physical and psychological dependence, as well as various emotional issues. With prolonged use, the brain adapts, requiring higher doses to achieve the same calming or euphoric effects—this is known as tolerance. However, this tolerance can decrease rapidly after stopping use, increasing the risk of overdose.
If you have developed a Soma addiction, recovery is possible. However, detoxing without professional and structured support can be difficult. In many cases, the safest option is a medically supervised detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, along with support from mental health professionals to address the psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personalised care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that address both co-occurring conditions and all underlying root causes of Soma addiction. In doing so, we can help you recover in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
CarisoprodolSoma.signsTitle: What are the signs of Soma addiction?
CarisoprodolSoma.signsDescription: It can be difficult to identify a Soma addiction, as substance misuse is not commonly associated with prescribed or over-the-counter medications.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Soma addiction
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom1Item1: Continuing to use Soma despite a wish to stop.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom1Item2: Needing to increase the dosage to achieve the desired effect.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom1Item3: Partaking in dangerous and risky behaviour to obtain the drug.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom1Item4: The development of withdrawal symptoms, such as tremors, insomnia, anxiety, nausea, sweating, and in severe cases, seizures when not taking the drug.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom1Item5: Isolating yourself from friends or loved ones.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Soma addiction?
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom2Item1: Impaired coordination such as clumsiness or inability to perform physical tasks effectively.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom2Item2: Dizziness, lightheadedness, trouble maintaining balance, and feeling faint.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom2Item3: Frequent headaches, often related to misuse or withdrawal.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom2Item4: Appearing to have much more or less energy than usual.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Soma addiction?
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom3Item1: An intense urge or compulsion to use Soma, even when inappropriate.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom3Item2: Feeling unable to relax, relieve pain, or function without Soma.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom3Item3: Mood swings: sudden irritability, depression, hostility, or agitation.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom3Item4: Nervousness, anxiety and restlessness, particularly when Soma is unavailable.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom3Item5: Cognitive impairments, such as memory loss, difficulty focusing, or confusion.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom3Item6: Feeling unusually happy, euphoric, or high’, especially when taking higher-than-prescribed doses.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Soma addiction?
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom4Item1: Using Soma despite the negative consequences to your finances, career and relationships.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom4Item2: Taking more Soma than prescribed or using it recreationally.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom4Item3: Visiting multiple doctors or chemists to obtain additional prescriptions.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom4Item4: Neglecting work, school, or family responsibilities due to drug use.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom4Item5: Risky behaviours: mixing Soma with alcohol or other drugs, increasing the risk of overdose.
CarisoprodolSoma.symptom4Item6: Concealing Soma pills or lying about usage.
CarisoprodolSoma.howWeTreatDescription:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that each recovery journey is deeply personal. While we cannot define exactly what your four-week stay will involve, we can assure you that every element of your treatment is designed to help you break the cycle of addiction. From withdrawal management to intensive therapy, every stage is fully personalised.
It is important to emphasise that we do not follow a rigid treatment model. Instead, we adopt a practical and flexible approach—whatever works best to help you achieve your goals.
Each day is carefully structured to include a range of therapies specifically selected to support your recovery and help you regain control of your life. Together, these therapies work to restore your physical, emotional, and psychological well-being.
CarisoprodolSoma.section1Description:
We understand that every journey is different, which is why we never take a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, we carefully tailor each programme to your specific needs. While we prioritise advanced methods and holistic care, your individual circumstances guide everything we do.
As part of your recovery, we provide a carefully supervised withdrawal process, incorporating medication-assisted treatment (MAT) and IV therapy to make detox as comfortable as possible. A key element of our approach is conducting in-depth assessments to identify the factors underlying your dependency. Based on these insights, we design a fully personalised treatment plan focused on long-term recovery.
Your programme may include therapies such as biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behaviour Therapy (DBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, EMDR, acupuncture, and art therapy.
In addition to addressing root causes, we also focus on the broader impact of Soma use, including any neurological or physical imbalances. Our integrated approach combines intensive psychotherapy, biochemical restoration, and complementary therapies to reduce stress, enhance well-being, and support a sustainable recovery.
CarisoprodolSoma.section2Description:
We are a residential addiction treatment centre that provides a dedicated team of professionals for each client.
Your recovery will be guided by a team of doctors, therapists, and addiction specialists. With highly personalised care, we offer continuous support to help manage withdrawal and guide you toward recovery. A live-in therapist is also available 24/7 for emotional support.
All treatments are delivered with the high standard of medical excellence that defines us.
CarisoprodolSoma.section3Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we design comprehensive and intensive treatment programmes tailored to each individual, based on principles of empathy and practicality. These needs may be physical and psychological—for example, if we identify nutritional deficiencies or signs of other addictions during the assessment, your programme will be adjusted accordingly.
We also consider practical needs, such as when a client needs to work or travel during their stay.
We provide treatment for Soma addiction, as well as co-occurring dependencies and mental health conditions, at our discreet treatment centre in Querétaro.

Codeine.conditionHeading: Codeine Addiction: How It Begins, What the Signs Are, and How We Treat It
Codeine.conditionDescription: Breaking free from codeine addiction is possible, and we’re here to help you heal. At Gabriel Arcángel, we take a holistic and practical approach, fully tailored to your needs. Our personalised programmes are designed to support you through a safe, expert-supervised withdrawal process while addressing the underlying causes of your addiction.
Codeine.whatIsDescription:
Codeine is an analgesic derived from morphine. Analgesics are narcotic painkillers—powerful medications that work by reducing the perception of pain. Codeine is one of the most commonly prescribed and easily accessible painkillers. It is typically used to treat mild to moderate pain, as well as to relieve coughs and diarrhoea. However, what often begins as a legitimate medical use can quickly spiral out of control, as tolerance builds over time, making it highly addictive.
Physical dependence occurs when an individual begins to rely excessively on codeine. The body becomes so accustomed to its presence that, in its absence, it feels as though something essential is missing, triggering withdrawal symptoms. Psychological dependence, on the other hand, develops when codeine is used to numb or escape emotional pain such as stress, loneliness, or trauma. In most cases, individuals experience a combination of both.
Over time, codeine abuse can seriously impact health. It increases the risk of respiratory depression, liver and kidney damage, seizures, and the development of other mental health conditions or substance use disorders.
Due to its addictive nature, detoxing from codeine on your own can be extremely difficult. In many cases, the safest approach is a medically supervised detox to manage physical withdrawal symptoms, alongside support from mental health professionals to address the psychological aspects.
Through our combination of medical, psychological, and personalised care, we are proud to be one of the few clinics that addresses both co-occurring conditions and every possible root cause of codeine addiction. In doing so, we can help you recover in a matter of weeks rather than months or years.
Codeine.signsTitle: What are the signs of Codeine addiction?
Codeine.signsDescription: Recognising the signs of codeine addiction is essential to intervene early and prevent serious consequences. This dependency can manifest through physical, emotional, and behavioural changes that are often subtle at first. Identifying these warning signs allows for timely action and seeking professional support. Detecting the problem early is key to achieving a safe and lasting recovery.
Codeine.symptoms1Title: Most common symptoms of Codeine addiction
Codeine.symptom1Item1: Wanting to cut down or stop using it but feeling unable to.
Codeine.symptom1Item2: Ingesting codeine even though the pain has subsided.
Codeine.symptom1Item3: Lying or exaggerating pain to get prescribed codeine.
Codeine.symptom1Item4: Taking codeine in response to stress, anxiety or boredom.
Codeine.symptom1Item5: Spending less time with family, friends or doing activities you once enjoyed.
Codeine.symptom1Item6: Going to different doctors or chemists in an attempt to get codeine.
Codeine.symptom1Item7: Needing ever larger doses to get the same pleasurable effects.
Codeine.symptoms2Title: What are the physical symptoms of Codeine addiction?
Codeine.symptoms3Title: What are the psychological symptoms of Codeine addiction?
Codeine.symptoms4Title: What are the behavioural symptoms of Codeine addiction?
Codeine.symptom4Item1: Frequent and unexplained absences from work, school, or social commitments.
Codeine.symptom4Item2: Financial difficulties from spending large sums on codeine.
Codeine.symptom4Item3: Neglect of responsibilities, relationships, or personal well-being.
Codeine.symptom4Item5: Feeling as though codeine has taken over your life.
Codeine.howWeTreatDescription:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we do not follow a predefined approach or rigid model when treating codeine addiction. Instead, we adopt a fully pragmatic and flexible methodology—whatever works best to help you achieve your goals. This means we fully support medication-assisted treatment (MAT) and do not require you to stop using other substances, such as nicotine or alcohol, if they are part of your current habits.
Every individual who comes to us receives a fully personalised treatment plan tailored to their specific needs. Our process includes a medically supervised and carefully monitored detox. We also provide supportive therapies and IV infusions to make the process as comfortable as possible. All therapies work together in harmony, addressing not only the physical aspects of addiction but also the emotional and psychological components.
Codeine.section1Description:
At Gabriel Arcángel, we understand that every recovery journey is unique, which is why we avoid one-size-fits-all approaches. Instead, we design each programme around your individual needs. While we prioritise advanced methods and holistic care, it is your personal circumstances that guide the entire process.
Our approach goes beyond simply treating symptoms—we aim to identify and address the deeper causes of your codeine dependence. Alongside a safe medical detox, we carry out comprehensive assessments that allow us to create a fully personalised treatment plan, targeting physical, emotional, and psychological factors.
This integrative approach includes therapies such as biofeedback, nutritional therapy, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR), sleep therapies, family therapy, and art therapy.
